parameters.name: objectGroup
parameters.description:
String object group name to put result into (allows rapid releasing resulting object handles
using `releaseObjectGroup`).

parameters.name: includeCommandLineAPI
parameters.description:
Specifies whether command line API should be available to the evaluated expression, defaults
to false.

parameters.name: silent
parameters.description:
In silent mode exceptions thrown during evaluation are not reported and do not pause
execution. Overrides `setPauseOnException` state.

parameters.name: returnByValue
parameters.description: Whether the result is expected to be a JSON object that should be sent by value.

parameters.name: generatePreview
parameters.description: Whether preview should be generated for the result.

parameters.name: throwOnSideEffect
parameters.description: Whether to throw an exception if side effect cannot be ruled out during evaluation.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Terminate execution after timing out (number of milliseconds).

returns.name: result
returns.description: Object wrapper for the evaluation result.

returns.name: exceptionDetails
returns.description: Exception details.

commands.name: getPossibleBreakpoints
commands.description:
Returns possible locations for breakpoint. scriptId in start and end range locations should be
the same.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of range to search possible breakpoint locations in.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description:
End of range to search possible breakpoint locations in (excluding). When not specified, end
of scripts is used as end of range.

parameters.name: restrictToFunction
parameters.description: Only consider locations which are in the same (non-nested) function as start.

returns.name: locations
returns.description: List of the possible breakpoint locations.

commands.name: getScriptSource
commands.description: Returns source for the script with given id.

parameters.name: scriptId
parameters.description: Id of the script to get source for.

returns.name: scriptSource
returns.description: Script source (empty in case of Wasm bytecode).

returns.name: bytecode
returns.description: Wasm bytecode. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

commands.name: getStackTrace
commands.description: Returns stack trace with given `stackTraceId`.

commands.name: getWasmBytecode
commands.description: This command is deprecated. Use getScriptSource instead.

parameters.name: scriptId
parameters.description: Id of the Wasm script to get source for.

returns.name: bytecode
returns.description: Script source. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

commands.name: nextWasmDisassemblyChunk
commands.description:
Disassemble the next chunk of lines for the module corresponding to the
stream. If disassembly is complete, this API will invalidate the streamId
and return an empty chunk. Any subsequent calls for the now invalid stream
will return errors.

returns.name: chunk
returns.description: The next chunk of disassembly.

commands.name: pause
commands.description: Stops on the next JavaScript statement.

parameters.name: parentStackTraceId
parameters.description: Debugger will pause when async call with given stack trace is started.

commands.name: removeBreakpoint
commands.description: Removes JavaScript breakpoint.

commands.name: restartFrame
commands.description:
Restarts particular call frame from the beginning. The old, deprecated
behavior of `restartFrame` is to stay paused and allow further CDP commands
after a restart was scheduled. This can cause problems with restarting, so
we now continue execution immediatly after it has been scheduled until we
reach the beginning of the restarted frame.

To stay back-wards compatible, `restartFrame` now expects a `mode`
parameter to be present. If the `mode` parameter is missing, `restartFrame`
errors out.

The various return values are deprecated and `callFrames` is always empty.
Use the call frames from the `Debugger#paused` events instead, that fires
once V8 pauses at the beginning of the restarted function.

parameters.name: callFrameId
parameters.description: Call frame identifier to evaluate on.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description:
The `mode` parameter must be present and set to 'StepInto', otherwise
`restartFrame` will error out.

returns.name: callFrames
returns.description: New stack trace.

returns.name: asyncStackTrace
returns.description: Async stack trace, if any.

returns.name: asyncStackTraceId
returns.description: Async stack trace, if any.

commands.name: resume
commands.description: Resumes JavaScript execution.

parameters.name: terminateOnResume
parameters.description:
Set to true to terminate execution upon resuming execution. In contrast
to Runtime.terminateExecution, this will allows to execute further
JavaScript (i.e. via evaluation) until execution of the paused code
is actually resumed, at which point termination is triggered.
If execution is currently not paused, this parameter has no effect.

commands.name: searchInContent
commands.description: Searches for given string in script content.

parameters.name: scriptId
parameters.description: Id of the script to search in.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: String to search for.

parameters.name: caseSensitive
parameters.description: If true, search is case sensitive.

parameters.name: isRegex
parameters.description: If true, treats string parameter as regex.

returns.name: result
returns.description: List of search matches.

commands.name: setAsyncCallStackDepth
commands.description: Enables or disables async call stacks tracking.

parameters.name: maxDepth
parameters.description:
Maximum depth of async call stacks. Setting to `0` will effectively disable collecting async
call stacks (default).

commands.name: setBlackboxedRanges
commands.description:
Makes backend skip steps in the script in blackboxed ranges. VM will try leave blacklisted
scripts by performing 'step in' several times, finally resorting to 'step out' if unsuccessful.
Positions array contains positions where blackbox state is changed. First interval isn't
blackboxed. Array should be sorted.

parameters.name: scriptId
parameters.description: Id of the script.

commands.name: setBlackboxExecutionContexts
commands.description:
Replace previous blackbox execution contexts with passed ones. Forces backend to skip
stepping/pausing in scripts in these execution contexts. VM will try to leave blackboxed script by
performing 'step in' several times, finally resorting to 'step out' if unsuccessful.

parameters.name: uniqueIds
parameters.description: Array of execution context unique ids for the debugger to ignore.

commands.name: setBlackboxPatterns
commands.description:
Replace previous blackbox patterns with passed ones. Forces backend to skip stepping/pausing in
scripts with url matching one of the patterns. VM will try to leave blackboxed script by
performing 'step in' several times, finally resorting to 'step out' if unsuccessful.

parameters.name: patterns
parameters.description: Array of regexps that will be used to check script url for blackbox state.

parameters.name: skipAnonymous
parameters.description: If true, also ignore scripts with no source url.

commands.name: setBreakpoint
commands.description: Sets JavaScript breakpoint at a given location.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: Location to set breakpoint in.

parameters.name: condition
parameters.description:
Expression to use as a breakpoint condition. When specified, debugger will only stop on the
breakpoint if this expression evaluates to true.

returns.name: breakpointId
returns.description: Id of the created breakpoint for further reference.

returns.name: actualLocation
returns.description: Location this breakpoint resolved into.

commands.name: setBreakpointByUrl
commands.description:
Sets JavaScript breakpoint at given location specified either by URL or URL regex. Once this
command is issued, all existing parsed scripts will have breakpoints resolved and returned in
`locations` property. Further matching script parsing will result in subsequent
`breakpointResolved` events issued. This logical breakpoint will survive page reloads.

parameters.name: lineNumber
parameters.description: Line number to set breakpoint at.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL of the resources to set breakpoint on.

parameters.name: urlRegex
parameters.description:
Regex pattern for the URLs of the resources to set breakpoints on. Either `url` or
`urlRegex` must be specified.

parameters.name: scriptHash
parameters.description: Script hash of the resources to set breakpoint on.

parameters.name: columnNumber
parameters.description: Offset in the line to set breakpoint at.

parameters.name: condition
parameters.description:
Expression to use as a breakpoint condition. When specified, debugger will only stop on the
breakpoint if this expression evaluates to true.

returns.name: breakpointId
returns.description: Id of the created breakpoint for further reference.

returns.name: locations
returns.description: List of the locations this breakpoint resolved into upon addition.

commands.name: setBreakpointOnFunctionCall
commands.description:
Sets JavaScript breakpoint before each call to the given function.
If another function was created from the same source as a given one,
calling it will also trigger the breakpoint.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: Function object id.

parameters.name: condition
parameters.description:
Expression to use as a breakpoint condition. When specified, debugger will
stop on the breakpoint if this expression evaluates to true.

returns.name: breakpointId
returns.description: Id of the created breakpoint for further reference.

commands.name: setBreakpointsActive
commands.description: Activates / deactivates all breakpoints on the page.

parameters.name: active
parameters.description: New value for breakpoints active state.

commands.name: setInstrumentationBreakpoint
commands.description: Sets instrumentation breakpoint.

parameters.name: instrumentation
parameters.description: Instrumentation name.

returns.name: breakpointId
returns.description: Id of the created breakpoint for further reference.

commands.name: setPauseOnExceptions
commands.description:
Defines pause on exceptions state. Can be set to stop on all exceptions, uncaught exceptions,
or caught exceptions, no exceptions. Initial pause on exceptions state is `none`.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Pause on exceptions mode.

commands.name: setReturnValue
commands.description: Changes return value in top frame. Available only at return break position.

parameters.name: newValue
parameters.description: New return value.

commands.name: setScriptSource
commands.description:
Edits JavaScript source live.

In general, functions that are currently on the stack can not be edited with
a single exception: If the edited function is the top-most stack frame and
that is the only activation of that function on the stack. In this case
the live edit will be successful and a `Debugger.restartFrame` for the
top-most function is automatically triggered.

parameters.name: scriptId
parameters.description: Id of the script to edit.

parameters.name: scriptSource
parameters.description: New content of the script.

parameters.name: dryRun
parameters.description:
If true the change will not actually be applied. Dry run may be used to get result
description without actually modifying the code.

parameters.name: allowTopFrameEditing
parameters.description:
If true, then `scriptSource` is allowed to change the function on top of the stack
as long as the top-most stack frame is the only activation of that function.

returns.name: callFrames
returns.description: New stack trace in case editing has happened while VM was stopped.

returns.name: stackChanged
returns.description: Whether current call stack  was modified after applying the changes.

returns.name: asyncStackTrace
returns.description: Async stack trace, if any.

returns.name: asyncStackTraceId
returns.description: Async stack trace, if any.

returns.name: status
returns.description:
Whether the operation was successful or not. Only `Ok` denotes a
successful live edit while the other enum variants denote why
the live edit failed.

returns.name: exceptionDetails
returns.description: Exception details if any. Only present when `status` is `CompileError`.

commands.name: setSkipAllPauses
commands.description: Makes page not interrupt on any pauses (breakpoint, exception, dom exception etc).

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: New value for skip pauses state.

commands.name: setVariableValue
commands.description:
Changes value of variable in a callframe. Object-based scopes are not supported and must be
mutated manually.

parameters.name: scopeNumber
parameters.description:
0-based number of scope as was listed in scope chain. Only 'local', 'closure' and 'catch'
scope types are allowed. Other scopes could be manipulated manually.

parameters.name: variableName
parameters.description: Variable name.

parameters.name: newValue
parameters.description: New variable value.

parameters.name: callFrameId
parameters.description: Id of callframe that holds variable.

commands.name: stepInto
commands.description: Steps into the function call.

parameters.name: breakOnAsyncCall
parameters.description:
Debugger will pause on the execution of the first async task which was scheduled
before next pause.

parameters.name: skipList
parameters.description: The skipList specifies location ranges that should be skipped on step into.

commands.name: stepOut
commands.description: Steps out of the function call.

commands.name: stepOver
commands.description: Steps over the statement.

parameters.name: skipList
parameters.description: The skipList specifies location ranges that should be skipped on step over.

events.name: breakpointResolved
events.description:
Fired when breakpoint is resolved to an actual script and location.
Deprecated in favor of `resolvedBreakpoints` in the `scriptParsed` event.

parameters.name: breakpointId
parameters.description: Breakpoint unique identifier.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: Actual breakpoint location.

events.name: paused
events.description: Fired when the virtual machine stopped on breakpoint or exception or any other stop criteria.

parameters.name: callFrames
parameters.description: Call stack the virtual machine stopped on.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Pause reason.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Object containing break-specific auxiliary properties.

parameters.name: hitBreakpoints
parameters.description: Hit breakpoints IDs

parameters.name: asyncStackTrace
parameters.description: Async stack trace, if any.

parameters.name: asyncStackTraceId
parameters.description: Async stack trace, if any.

parameters.name: asyncCallStackTraceId
parameters.description: Never present, will be removed.

events.name: resumed
events.description: Fired when the virtual machine resumed execution.

events.name: scriptFailedToParse
events.description: Fired when virtual machine fails to parse the script.

parameters.name: scriptId
parameters.description: Identifier of the script parsed.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL or name of the script parsed (if any).

parameters.name: startLine
parameters.description: Line offset of the script within the resource with given URL (for script tags).

parameters.name: startColumn
parameters.description: Column offset of the script within the resource with given URL.

parameters.name: endLine
parameters.description: Last line of the script.

parameters.name: endColumn
parameters.description: Length of the last line of the script.

parameters.name: executionContextId
parameters.description: Specifies script creation context.

parameters.name: hash
parameters.description: Content hash of the script, SHA-256.

parameters.name: buildId
parameters.description: For Wasm modules, the content of the `build_id` custom section. For JavaScript the `debugId` magic comment.

parameters.name: executionContextAuxData
parameters.description: Embedder-specific auxiliary data likely matching {isDefault: boolean, type: 'default'|'isolated'|'worker', frameId: string}

parameters.name: sourceMapURL
parameters.description: URL of source map associated with script (if any).

parameters.name: hasSourceURL
parameters.description: True, if this script has sourceURL.

parameters.name: isModule
parameters.description: True, if this script is ES6 module.

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: This script length.

parameters.name: stackTrace
parameters.description: JavaScript top stack frame of where the script parsed event was triggered if available.

parameters.name: codeOffset
parameters.description: If the scriptLanguage is WebAssembly, the code section offset in the module.

parameters.name: scriptLanguage
parameters.description: The language of the script.

parameters.name: embedderName
parameters.description: The name the embedder supplied for this script.

events.name: scriptParsed
events.description:
Fired when virtual machine parses script. This event is also fired for all known and uncollected
scripts upon enabling debugger.

parameters.name: scriptId
parameters.description: Identifier of the script parsed.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL or name of the script parsed (if any).

parameters.name: startLine
parameters.description: Line offset of the script within the resource with given URL (for script tags).

parameters.name: startColumn
parameters.description: Column offset of the script within the resource with given URL.

parameters.name: endLine
parameters.description: Last line of the script.

parameters.name: endColumn
parameters.description: Length of the last line of the script.

parameters.name: executionContextId
parameters.description: Specifies script creation context.

parameters.name: hash
parameters.description: Content hash of the script, SHA-256.

parameters.name: buildId
parameters.description: For Wasm modules, the content of the `build_id` custom section. For JavaScript the `debugId` magic comment.

parameters.name: executionContextAuxData
parameters.description: Embedder-specific auxiliary data likely matching {isDefault: boolean, type: 'default'|'isolated'|'worker', frameId: string}

parameters.name: isLiveEdit
parameters.description: True, if this script is generated as a result of the live edit operation.

parameters.name: sourceMapURL
parameters.description: URL of source map associated with script (if any).

parameters.name: hasSourceURL
parameters.description: True, if this script has sourceURL.

parameters.name: isModule
parameters.description: True, if this script is ES6 module.

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: This script length.

parameters.name: stackTrace
parameters.description: JavaScript top stack frame of where the script parsed event was triggered if available.

parameters.name: codeOffset
parameters.description: If the scriptLanguage is WebAssembly, the code section offset in the module.

parameters.name: scriptLanguage
parameters.description: The language of the script.

parameters.name: debugSymbols
parameters.description: If the scriptLanguage is WebAssembly, the source of debug symbols for the module.

parameters.name: embedderName
parameters.description: The name the embedder supplied for this script.

parameters.name: resolvedBreakpoints
parameters.description:
The list of set breakpoints in this script if calls to `setBreakpointByUrl`
matches this script's URL or hash. Clients that use this list can ignore the
`breakpointResolved` event. They are equivalent.

properties.name: scriptId
properties.description: Script identifier as reported in the `Debugger.scriptParsed`.

properties.name: lineNumber
properties.description: Line number in the script (0-based).

properties.name: columnNumber
properties.description: Column number in the script (0-based).

types.name: Debugger.BreakpointId
types.description: Breakpoint identifier.

types.name: Debugger.CallFrame
types.description: JavaScript call frame. Array of call frames form the call stack.

properties.name: callFrameId
properties.description: Call frame identifier. This identifier is only valid while the virtual machine is paused.

properties.name: functionName
properties.description: Name of the JavaScript function called on this call frame.

properties.name: functionLocation
properties.description: Location in the source code.

properties.name: location
properties.description: Location in the source code.

properties.name: url
properties.description:
JavaScript script name or url.
Deprecated in favor of using the `location.scriptId` to resolve the URL via a previously
sent `Debugger.scriptParsed` event.

properties.name: scopeChain
properties.description: Scope chain for this call frame.

properties.name: this
properties.description: `this` object for this call frame.

properties.name: returnValue
properties.description: The value being returned, if the function is at return point.

properties.name: canBeRestarted
properties.description:
Valid only while the VM is paused and indicates whether this frame
can be restarted or not. Note that a `true` value here does not
guarantee that Debugger#restartFrame with this CallFrameId will be
successful, but it is very likely.

types.name: Debugger.CallFrameId
types.description: Call frame identifier.

types.name: Debugger.DebugSymbols
types.description: Debug symbols available for a wasm script.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Type of the debug symbols.

properties.name: externalURL
properties.description: URL of the external symbol source.

types.name: Debugger.Location
types.description: Location in the source code.

properties.name: scriptId
properties.description: Script identifier as reported in the `Debugger.scriptParsed`.

properties.name: lineNumber
properties.description: Line number in the script (0-based).

properties.name: columnNumber
properties.description: Column number in the script (0-based).

types.name: Debugger.LocationRange
types.description: Location range within one script.

properties.name: breakpointId
properties.description: Breakpoint unique identifier.

properties.name: location
properties.description: Actual breakpoint location.

types.name: Debugger.Scope
types.description: Scope description.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Scope type.

properties.name: object
properties.description:
Object representing the scope. For `global` and `with` scopes it represents the actual
object; for the rest of the scopes, it is artificial transient object enumerating scope
variables as its properties.

properties.name: startLocation
properties.description: Location in the source code where scope starts

properties.name: endLocation
properties.description: Location in the source code where scope ends

types.name: Debugger.ScriptLanguage
types.description: Enum of possible script languages.

types.name: Debugger.ScriptPosition
types.description: Location in the source code.

types.name: Debugger.SearchMatch
types.description: Search match for resource.

properties.name: lineNumber
properties.description: Line number in resource content.

properties.name: lineContent
properties.description: Line with match content.

properties.name: lines
properties.description: The next chunk of disassembled lines.

properties.name: bytecodeOffsets
properties.description: The bytecode offsets describing the start of each line.

commands.name: cancelPrompt
commands.description: Cancel a prompt in response to a DeviceAccess.deviceRequestPrompted event.

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disable events in this domain.

commands.name: enable
commands.description: Enable events in this domain.

commands.name: selectPrompt
commands.description: Select a device in response to a DeviceAccess.deviceRequestPrompted event.

events.name: deviceRequestPrompted
events.description:
A device request opened a user prompt to select a device. Respond with the
selectPrompt or cancelPrompt command.

types.name: DeviceAccess.DeviceId
types.description: A device id.

types.name: DeviceAccess.PromptDevice
types.description: Device information displayed in a user prompt to select a device.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Display name as it appears in a device request user prompt.

types.name: DeviceAccess.RequestId
types.description: Device request id.

commands.name: clearDeviceOrientationOverride
commands.description: Clears the overridden Device Orientation.

commands.name: setDeviceOrientationOverride
commands.description: Overrides the Device Orientation.

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.description: Mock alpha

parameters.name: beta
parameters.description: Mock beta

parameters.name: gamma
parameters.description: Mock gamma

domains.name: DOM
domains.description:
This domain exposes DOM read/write operations. Each DOM Node is represented with its mirror object
that has an `id`. This `id` can be used to get additional information on the Node, resolve it into
the JavaScript object wrapper, etc. It is important that client receives DOM events only for the
nodes that are known to the client. Backend keeps track of the nodes that were sent to the client
and never sends the same node twice. It is client's responsibility to collect information about
the nodes that were sent to the client. Note that `iframe` owner elements will return
corresponding document elements as their child nodes.

commands.name: collectClassNamesFromSubtree
commands.description: Collects class names for the node with given id and all of it's child nodes.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Id of the node to collect class names.

returns.name: classNames
returns.description: Class name list.

commands.name: copyTo
commands.description:
Creates a deep copy of the specified node and places it into the target container before the
given anchor.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Id of the node to copy.

parameters.name: targetNodeId
parameters.description: Id of the element to drop the copy into.

parameters.name: insertBeforeNodeId
parameters.description:
Drop the copy before this node (if absent, the copy becomes the last child of
`targetNodeId`).

returns.name: nodeId
returns.description: Id of the node clone.

commands.name: describeNode
commands.description:
Describes node given its id, does not require domain to be enabled. Does not start tracking any
objects, can be used for automation.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the node.

parameters.name: backendNodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the backend node.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: JavaScript object id of the node wrapper.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.description:
The maximum depth at which children should be retrieved, defaults to 1. Use -1 for the
entire subtree or provide an integer larger than 0.

parameters.name: pierce
parameters.description:
Whether or not iframes and shadow roots should be traversed when returning the subtree
(default is false).

returns.name: node
returns.description: Node description.

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables DOM agent for the given page.

commands.name: discardSearchResults
commands.description:
Discards search results from the session with the given id. `getSearchResults` should no longer
be called for that search.

parameters.name: searchId
parameters.description: Unique search session identifier.

commands.name: enable
commands.description: Enables DOM agent for the given page.

parameters.name: includeWhitespace
parameters.description: Whether to include whitespaces in the children array of returned Nodes.

commands.name: focus
commands.description: Focuses the given element.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the node.

parameters.name: backendNodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the backend node.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: JavaScript object id of the node wrapper.

commands.name: forceShowPopover
commands.description:
When enabling, this API force-opens the popover identified by nodeId
and keeps it open until disabled.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Id of the popover HTMLElement

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description:
If true, opens the popover and keeps it open. If false, closes the
popover if it was previously force-opened.

returns.name: nodeIds
returns.description: List of popovers that were closed in order to respect popover stacking order.

commands.name: getAnchorElement
commands.description:
Returns the target anchor element of the given anchor query according to
https://www.w3.org/TR/css-anchor-position-1/#target.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Id of the positioned element from which to find the anchor.

parameters.name: anchorSpecifier
parameters.description:
An optional anchor specifier, as defined in
https://www.w3.org/TR/css-anchor-position-1/#anchor-specifier.
If not provided, it will return the implicit anchor element for
the given positioned element.

returns.name: nodeId
returns.description: The anchor element of the given anchor query.

commands.name: getAttributes
commands.description: Returns attributes for the specified node.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Id of the node to retrieve attributes for.

returns.name: attributes
returns.description: An interleaved array of node attribute names and values.

commands.name: getBoxModel
commands.description: Returns boxes for the given node.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the node.

parameters.name: backendNodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the backend node.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: JavaScript object id of the node wrapper.

returns.name: model
returns.description: Box model for the node.

commands.name: getContainerForNode
commands.description:
Returns the query container of the given node based on container query
conditions: containerName, physical and logical axes, and whether it queries
scroll-state or anchored elements. If no axes are provided and
queriesScrollState is false, the style container is returned, which is the
direct parent or the closest element with a matching container-name.

returns.name: nodeId
returns.description: The container node for the given node, or null if not found.

commands.name: getContentQuads
commands.description:
Returns quads that describe node position on the page. This method
might return multiple quads for inline nodes.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the node.

parameters.name: backendNodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the backend node.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: JavaScript object id of the node wrapper.

returns.name: quads
returns.description: Quads that describe node layout relative to viewport.

commands.name: getDetachedDomNodes
commands.description: Returns list of detached nodes

returns.name: detachedNodes
returns.description: The list of detached nodes

commands.name: getDocument
commands.description:
Returns the root DOM node (and optionally the subtree) to the caller.
Implicitly enables the DOM domain events for the current target.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.description:
The maximum depth at which children should be retrieved, defaults to 1. Use -1 for the
entire subtree or provide an integer larger than 0.

parameters.name: pierce
parameters.description:
Whether or not iframes and shadow roots should be traversed when returning the subtree
(default is false).

returns.name: root
returns.description: Resulting node.

commands.name: getElementByRelation
commands.description: Returns the NodeId of the matched element according to certain relations.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Id of the node from which to query the relation.

parameters.name: relation
parameters.description: Type of relation to get.

returns.name: nodeId
returns.description: NodeId of the element matching the queried relation.

commands.name: getFileInfo
commands.description:
Returns file information for the given
File wrapper.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: JavaScript object id of the node wrapper.

commands.name: getFlattenedDocument
commands.description:
Returns the root DOM node (and optionally the subtree) to the caller.
Deprecated, as it is not designed to work well with the rest of the DOM agent.
Use DOMSnapshot.captureSnapshot instead.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.description:
The maximum depth at which children should be retrieved, defaults to 1. Use -1 for the
entire subtree or provide an integer larger than 0.

parameters.name: pierce
parameters.description:
Whether or not iframes and shadow roots should be traversed when returning the subtree
(default is false).

returns.name: nodes

commands.name: getSearchResults

identifier.

returns.name: nodeIds

commands.name: getTopLayerElements

Returns NodeIds of current top layer elements.
Top layer is rendered closest to the user within a viewport, therefore its elements always
appear on top of all other content.

returns.name: nodeIds

commands.name: requestChildNodes
commands.description:
Requests that children of the node with given id are returned to the caller in form of
`setChildNodes` events where not only immediate children are retrieved, but all children down to
the specified depth.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Id of the node to get children for.

parameters.description:
The maximum depth at which children should be retrieved, defaults to 1. Use -1 for the
entire subtree or provide an integer larger than 0.

parameters.name: pierce
parameters.description:
Whether or not iframes and shadow roots should be traversed when returning the sub-tree
(default is false).

commands.description:
Requests that the node is sent to the caller given the JavaScript node object reference. All
nodes that form the path from the node to the root are also sent to the client as a series of
`setChildNodes` notifications.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: JavaScript object id to convert into node.

returns.name: nodeId

commands.description: Resolves the JavaScript node object for a given NodeId or BackendNodeId.

parameters.description: Id of the node to resolve.

returns.name: object
returns.description: JavaScript object wrapper for given node.

commands.name: scrollIntoViewIfNeeded
commands.description:
Scrolls the specified rect of the given node into view if not already visible.
Note: exactly one between nodeId, backendNodeId and objectId should be passed
to identify the node.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the node.

parameters.name: backendNodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the backend node.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: JavaScript object id of the node wrapper.

parameters.name: rect
parameters.description:
The rect to be scrolled into view, relative to the node's border box, in CSS pixels.
When omitted, center of the node will be used, similar to Element.scrollIntoView.

domains.name: DOMDebugger
domains.description:
DOM debugging allows setting breakpoints on particular DOM operations and events. JavaScript
execution will stop on these operations as if there was a regular breakpoint set.

commands.name: getEventListeners
commands.description: Returns event listeners of the given object.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: Identifier of the object to return listeners for.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.description:
The maximum depth at which Node children should be retrieved, defaults to 1. Use -1 for the
entire subtree or provide an integer larger than 0.

parameters.name: pierce
parameters.description:
Whether or not iframes and shadow roots should be traversed when returning the subtree
(default is false). Reports listeners for all contexts if pierce is enabled.

returns.name: listeners
returns.description: Array of relevant listeners.

commands.description: Removes breakpoint on particular native event.

commands.name: removeXHRBreakpoint
commands.description: Removes breakpoint from XMLHttpRequest.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Resource URL substring.

commands.description: Sets breakpoint on particular CSP violations.

parameters.name: violationTypes
parameters.description: CSP Violations to stop upon.

commands.name: setDOMBreakpoint
commands.description: Sets breakpoint on particular operation with DOM.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the node to set breakpoint on.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of the operation to stop upon.

commands.name: setEventListenerBreakpoint

parameters.name: eventName
parameters.description: DOM Event name to stop on (any DOM event will do).

parameters.name: targetName
parameters.description:
EventTarget interface name to stop on. If equal to `"*"` or not provided, will stop on any
EventTarget.

commands.name: setInstrumentationBreakpoint
commands.description: Sets breakpoint on particular native event.

parameters.name: eventName
parameters.description: Instrumentation name to stop on.

commands.name: setXHRBreakpoint
commands.description: Sets breakpoint on XMLHttpRequest.

parameters.name: url

domains.name: DOMSnapshot
domains.description: This domain facilitates obtaining document snapshots with DOM, layout, and style information.

commands.name: captureSnapshot
commands.description:
Returns a document snapshot, including the full DOM tree of the root node (including iframes,
template contents, and imported documents) in a flattened array, as well as layout and
white-listed computed style information for the nodes. Shadow DOM in the returned DOM tree is
flattened.

parameters.name: computedStyles
parameters.description: Whitelist of computed styles to return.

parameters.name: includePaintOrder
parameters.description: Whether to include layout object paint orders into the snapshot.

parameters.name: includeDOMRects
parameters.description: Whether to include DOM rectangles (offsetRects, clientRects, scrollRects) into the snapshot

parameters.name: includeBlendedBackgroundColors
parameters.description:
Whether to include blended background colors in the snapshot (default: false).
Blended background color is achieved by blending background colors of all elements
that overlap with the current element.

parameters.name: includeTextColorOpacities
parameters.description:
Whether to include text color opacity in the snapshot (default: false).
An element might have the opacity property set that affects the text color of the element.
The final text color opacity is computed based on the opacity of all overlapping elements.

returns.name: documents
returns.description: The nodes in the DOM tree. The DOMNode at index 0 corresponds to the root document.

returns.name: strings
returns.description: Shared string table that all string properties refer to with indexes.

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables DOM snapshot agent for the given page.

commands.name: enable
commands.description: Enables DOM snapshot agent for the given page.

commands.name: getSnapshot
commands.description:
Returns a document snapshot, including the full DOM tree of the root node (including iframes,
template contents, and imported documents) in a flattened array, as well as layout and
white-listed computed style information for the nodes. Shadow DOM in the returned DOM tree is
flattened.

parameters.name: computedStyleWhitelist
parameters.description: Whitelist of computed styles to return.

parameters.name: includeEventListeners
parameters.description: Whether or not to retrieve details of DOM listeners (default false).

parameters.name: includePaintOrder
parameters.description: Whether to determine and include the paint order index of LayoutTreeNodes (default false).

parameters.name: includeUserAgentShadowTree
parameters.description: Whether to include UA shadow tree in the snapshot (default false).

returns.name: domNodes
returns.description: The nodes in the DOM tree. The DOMNode at index 0 corresponds to the root document.

returns.name: layoutTreeNodes
returns.description: The nodes in the layout tree.

returns.name: computedStyles
returns.description: Whitelisted ComputedStyle properties for each node in the layout tree.

types.name: DOMSnapshot.ArrayOfStrings
types.description: Index of the string in the strings table.

types.name: DOMSnapshot.ComputedStyle
types.description: A subset of the full ComputedStyle as defined by the request whitelist.

properties.name: properties
properties.description: Name/value pairs of computed style properties.

types.name: DOMSnapshot.DocumentSnapshot
types.description: Document snapshot.

properties.name: documentURL
properties.description: Document URL that `Document` or `FrameOwner` node points to.

properties.name: title
properties.description: Document title.

properties.name: baseURL
properties.description: Base URL that `Document` or `FrameOwner` node uses for URL completion.

properties.name: contentLanguage
properties.description: Contains the document's content language.

properties.name: encodingName
properties.description: Contains the document's character set encoding.

properties.name: publicId
properties.description: `DocumentType` node's publicId.

properties.name: systemId
properties.description: `DocumentType` node's systemId.

properties.name: frameId
properties.description: Frame ID for frame owner elements and also for the document node.

properties.name: nodes
properties.description: A table with dom nodes.

properties.name: layout
properties.description: The nodes in the layout tree.

properties.name: textBoxes
properties.description: The post-layout inline text nodes.

properties.name: scrollOffsetX
properties.description: Horizontal scroll offset.

properties.name: scrollOffsetY
properties.description: Vertical scroll offset.

properties.name: contentWidth
properties.description: Document content width.

properties.name: contentHeight
properties.description: Document content height.

types.name: DOMSnapshot.DOMNode
types.description: A Node in the DOM tree.

properties.name: nodeType
properties.description: `Node`'s nodeType.

properties.name: nodeName
properties.description: `Node`'s nodeName.

properties.name: nodeValue
properties.description: `Node`'s nodeValue.

properties.name: textValue
properties.description: Only set for textarea elements, contains the text value.

properties.name: inputValue
properties.description: Only set for input elements, contains the input's associated text value.

properties.name: inputChecked
properties.description: Only set for radio and checkbox input elements, indicates if the element has been checked

properties.name: optionSelected
properties.description: Only set for option elements, indicates if the element has been selected

properties.name: backendNodeId
properties.description: `Node`'s id, corresponds to DOM.Node.backendNodeId.

properties.name: childNodeIndexes
properties.description:
The indexes of the node's child nodes in the `domNodes` array returned by `getSnapshot`, if
any.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Attributes of an `Element` node.

properties.name: pseudoElementIndexes
properties.description:
Indexes of pseudo elements associated with this node in the `domNodes` array returned by
`getSnapshot`, if any.

properties.name: layoutNodeIndex
properties.description:
The index of the node's related layout tree node in the `layoutTreeNodes` array returned by
`getSnapshot`, if any.

properties.name: documentURL
properties.description: Document URL that `Document` or `FrameOwner` node points to.

properties.name: baseURL
properties.description: Base URL that `Document` or `FrameOwner` node uses for URL completion.

properties.name: contentLanguage
properties.description: Only set for documents, contains the document's content language.

properties.name: documentEncoding
properties.description: Only set for documents, contains the document's character set encoding.

properties.name: publicId
properties.description: `DocumentType` node's publicId.

properties.name: systemId
properties.description: `DocumentType` node's systemId.

properties.name: frameId
properties.description: Frame ID for frame owner elements and also for the document node.

properties.name: contentDocumentIndex
properties.description:
The index of a frame owner element's content document in the `domNodes` array returned by
`getSnapshot`, if any.

properties.name: pseudoType
properties.description: Type of a pseudo element node.

properties.name: shadowRootType
properties.description: Shadow root type.

properties.name: isClickable
properties.description:
Whether this DOM node responds to mouse clicks. This includes nodes that have had click
event listeners attached via JavaScript as well as anchor tags that naturally navigate when
clicked.

properties.name: eventListeners
properties.description: Details of the node's event listeners, if any.

properties.name: currentSourceURL
properties.description: The selected url for nodes with a srcset attribute.

properties.name: originURL
properties.description: The url of the script (if any) that generates this node.

properties.name: scrollOffsetX
properties.description: Scroll offsets, set when this node is a Document.

types.name: DOMSnapshot.InlineTextBox
types.description:
Details of post layout rendered text positions. The exact layout should not be regarded as
stable and may change between versions.

properties.name: boundingBox
properties.description: The bounding box in document coordinates. Note that scroll offset of the document is ignored.

properties.name: startCharacterIndex
properties.description:
The starting index in characters, for this post layout textbox substring. Characters that
would be represented as a surrogate pair in UTF-16 have length 2.

properties.name: numCharacters
properties.description:
The number of characters in this post layout textbox substring. Characters that would be
represented as a surrogate pair in UTF-16 have length 2.

types.name: DOMSnapshot.LayoutTreeNode
types.description: Details of an element in the DOM tree with a LayoutObject.

properties.name: domNodeIndex
properties.description: The index of the related DOM node in the `domNodes` array returned by `getSnapshot`.

properties.name: boundingBox
properties.description: The bounding box in document coordinates. Note that scroll offset of the document is ignored.

properties.name: layoutText
properties.description: Contents of the LayoutText, if any.

properties.name: inlineTextNodes
properties.description: The post-layout inline text nodes, if any.

properties.name: styleIndex
properties.description: Index into the `computedStyles` array returned by `getSnapshot`.

properties.name: paintOrder
properties.description:
Global paint order index, which is determined by the stacking order of the nodes. Nodes
that are painted together will have the same index. Only provided if includePaintOrder in
getSnapshot was true.

properties.name: isStackingContext
properties.description: Set to true to indicate the element begins a new stacking context.

types.name: DOMSnapshot.LayoutTreeSnapshot
types.description: Table of details of an element in the DOM tree with a LayoutObject.

properties.name: nodeIndex
properties.description: Index of the corresponding node in the `NodeTreeSnapshot` array returned by `captureSnapshot`.

properties.name: styles
properties.description: Array of indexes specifying computed style strings, filtered according to the `computedStyles` parameter passed to `captureSnapshot`.

properties.name: bounds
properties.description: The absolute position bounding box.

properties.name: text
properties.description: Contents of the LayoutText, if any.

properties.name: stackingContexts
properties.description: Stacking context information.

properties.name: paintOrders
properties.description:
Global paint order index, which is determined by the stacking order of the nodes. Nodes
that are painted together will have the same index. Only provided if includePaintOrder in
captureSnapshot was true.

properties.name: offsetRects
properties.description: The offset rect of nodes. Only available when includeDOMRects is set to true

properties.name: scrollRects
properties.description: The scroll rect of nodes. Only available when includeDOMRects is set to true

properties.name: clientRects
properties.description: The client rect of nodes. Only available when includeDOMRects is set to true

properties.name: blendedBackgroundColors
properties.description: The list of background colors that are blended with colors of overlapping elements.

properties.name: textColorOpacities
properties.description: The list of computed text opacities.

types.name: DOMSnapshot.NameValue
types.description: A name/value pair.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Attribute/property name.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Attribute/property value.

types.name: DOMSnapshot.NodeTreeSnapshot
types.description: Table containing nodes.

properties.name: parentIndex
properties.description: Parent node index.

properties.name: nodeType
properties.description: `Node`'s nodeType.

properties.name: shadowRootType
properties.description: Type of the shadow root the `Node` is in. String values are equal to the `ShadowRootType` enum.

properties.name: nodeName
properties.description: `Node`'s nodeName.

properties.name: nodeValue
properties.description: `Node`'s nodeValue.

properties.name: backendNodeId
properties.description: `Node`'s id, corresponds to DOM.Node.backendNodeId.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Attributes of an `Element` node. Flatten name, value pairs.

properties.name: textValue
properties.description: Only set for textarea elements, contains the text value.

properties.name: inputValue
properties.description: Only set for input elements, contains the input's associated text value.

properties.name: inputChecked
properties.description: Only set for radio and checkbox input elements, indicates if the element has been checked

properties.name: optionSelected
properties.description: Only set for option elements, indicates if the element has been selected

properties.name: contentDocumentIndex
properties.description: The index of the document in the list of the snapshot documents.

properties.name: pseudoType
properties.description: Type of a pseudo element node.

properties.name: pseudoIdentifier
properties.description:
Pseudo element identifier for this node. Only present if there is a
valid pseudoType.

properties.name: isClickable
properties.description:
Whether this DOM node responds to mouse clicks. This includes nodes that have had click
event listeners attached via JavaScript as well as anchor tags that naturally navigate when
clicked.

properties.name: currentSourceURL
properties.description: The selected url for nodes with a srcset attribute.

properties.name: originURL
properties.description: The url of the script (if any) that generates this node.

types.name: DOMSnapshot.RareStringData
types.description: Data that is only present on rare nodes.

types.name: DOMSnapshot.StringIndex
types.description: Index of the string in the strings table.

types.name: DOMSnapshot.TextBoxSnapshot
types.description:
Table of details of the post layout rendered text positions. The exact layout should not be regarded as
stable and may change between versions.

properties.name: layoutIndex
properties.description: Index of the layout tree node that owns this box collection.

properties.name: bounds
properties.description: The absolute position bounding box.

properties.name: start
properties.description:
The starting index in characters, for this post layout textbox substring. Characters that
would be represented as a surrogate pair in UTF-16 have length 2.

properties.name: length
properties.description:
The number of characters in this post layout textbox substring. Characters that would be
represented as a surrogate pair in UTF-16 have length 2.

domains.name: DOMStorage
domains.description: Query and modify DOM storage.

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables storage tracking, prevents storage events from being sent to the client.

commands.name: enable
commands.description: Enables storage tracking, storage events will now be delivered to the client.

types.name: DOMStorage.Item
types.description: DOM Storage item.

types.name: DOMStorage.StorageId
types.description: DOM Storage identifier.

properties.name: securityOrigin
properties.description: Security origin for the storage.

properties.name: storageKey
properties.description: Represents a key by which DOM Storage keys its CachedStorageAreas

properties.name: isLocalStorage
properties.description: Whether the storage is local storage (not session storage).

domains.name: Emulation
domains.description: This domain emulates different environments for the page.

commands.name: addScreen
commands.description: Add a new screen to the device. Only supported in headless mode.

parameters.name: left
parameters.description: Offset of the left edge of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Offset of the top edge of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: The width of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: The height of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: workAreaInsets
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's work area. Default is entire screen.

parameters.name: devicePixelRatio
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's device pixel ratio. Default is 1.

parameters.name: rotation
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's rotation angle. Available values are 0, 90, 180 and 270. Default is 0.

parameters.name: colorDepth
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's color depth in bits. Default is 24.

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: Specifies the descriptive label for the screen. Default is none.

parameters.name: isInternal
parameters.description: Indicates whether the screen is internal to the device or external, attached to the device. Default is false.

commands.name: canEmulate
commands.description: Tells whether emulation is supported.

returns.name: result
returns.description: True if emulation is supported.

commands.name: clearDeviceMetricsOverride
commands.description: Clears the overridden device metrics.

commands.name: clearDevicePostureOverride
commands.description:
Clears a device posture override set with either setDeviceMetricsOverride()
or setDevicePostureOverride() and starts using posture information from the
platform again.
Does nothing if no override is set.

commands.name: clearDisplayFeaturesOverride
commands.description:
Clears the display features override set with either setDeviceMetricsOverride()
or setDisplayFeaturesOverride() and starts using display features from the
platform again.
Does nothing if no override is set.

commands.name: clearGeolocationOverride
commands.description: Clears the overridden Geolocation Position and Error.

commands.name: clearIdleOverride
commands.description: Clears Idle state overrides.

commands.name: getScreenInfos
commands.description:
Returns device's screen configuration. In headful mode, the physical screens configuration is returned,
whereas in headless mode, a virtual headless screen configuration is provided instead.

commands.name: removeScreen
commands.description: Remove screen from the device. Only supported in headless mode.

commands.name: resetPageScaleFactor
commands.description: Requests that page scale factor is reset to initial values.

commands.name: setAutoDarkModeOverride
commands.description: Automatically render all web contents using a dark theme.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description:
Whether to enable or disable automatic dark mode.
If not specified, any existing override will be cleared.

commands.name: setAutomationOverride
commands.description: Allows overriding the automation flag.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether the override should be enabled.

commands.name: setCPUThrottlingRate
commands.description: Enables CPU throttling to emulate slow CPUs.

parameters.name: rate
parameters.description: Throttling rate as a slowdown factor (1 is no throttle, 2 is 2x slowdown, etc).

commands.name: setDataSaverOverride
commands.description: Override the value of navigator.connection.saveData

parameters.name: dataSaverEnabled
parameters.description: Override value. Omitting the parameter disables the override.

commands.name: setDefaultBackgroundColorOverride
commands.description:
Sets or clears an override of the default background color of the frame. This override is used
if the content does not specify one.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description:
RGBA of the default background color. If not specified, any existing override will be
cleared.

commands.name: setDeviceMetricsOverride
commands.description:
Overrides the values of device screen dimensions (window.screen.width, window.screen.height,
window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight, and "device-width"/"device-height"-related CSS media
query results).

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: Overriding width value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000). 0 disables the override.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: Overriding height value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000). 0 disables the override.

parameters.name: deviceScaleFactor
parameters.description: Overriding device scale factor value. 0 disables the override.

parameters.name: mobile
parameters.description:
Whether to emulate mobile device. This includes viewport meta tag, overlay scrollbars, text
autosizing and more.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Scale to apply to resulting view image.

parameters.name: screenWidth
parameters.description: Overriding screen width value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).

parameters.name: screenHeight
parameters.description: Overriding screen height value in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).

parameters.name: positionX
parameters.description: Overriding view X position on screen in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).

parameters.name: positionY
parameters.description: Overriding view Y position on screen in pixels (minimum 0, maximum 10000000).

parameters.name: dontSetVisibleSize
parameters.description: Do not set visible view size, rely upon explicit setVisibleSize call.

parameters.name: screenOrientation
parameters.description: Screen orientation override.

parameters.name: viewport
parameters.description:
If set, the visible area of the page will be overridden to this viewport. This viewport
change is not observed by the page, e.g. viewport-relative elements do not change positions.

parameters.name: displayFeature
parameters.description:
If set, the display feature of a multi-segment screen. If not set, multi-segment support
is turned-off.
Deprecated, use Emulation.setDisplayFeaturesOverride.

parameters.name: devicePosture
parameters.description:
If set, the posture of a foldable device. If not set the posture is set
to continuous.
Deprecated, use Emulation.setDevicePostureOverride.

parameters.name: scrollbarType
parameters.description: Scrollbar type. Default: `default`.

parameters.name: screenOrientationLockEmulation
parameters.description:
If set to true, enables screen orientation lock emulation, which
intercepts screen.orientation.lock() calls from the page and reports
orientation changes via screenOrientationLockChanged events. This is
useful for emulating mobile device orientation lock behavior in
responsive design mode.

commands.name: setDevicePostureOverride
commands.description:
Start reporting the given posture value to the Device Posture API.
This override can also be set in setDeviceMetricsOverride().

parameters.name: imageTypes
parameters.description: Image types to disable.

commands.name: setDisplayFeaturesOverride
commands.description:
Start using the given display features to pupulate the Viewport Segments API.
This override can also be set in setDeviceMetricsOverride().

parameters.name: disabled
parameters.description: Whether document.coookie API should be disabled.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether touch emulation based on mouse input should be enabled.

parameters.name: configuration
parameters.description: Touch/gesture events configuration. Default: current platform.

commands.name: setEmulatedMedia
commands.description: Emulates the given media type or media feature for CSS media queries.

parameters.name: media
parameters.description: Media type to emulate. Empty string disables the override.

parameters.name: features
parameters.description: Media features to emulate.

commands.name: setEmulatedOSTextScale
commands.description: Emulates the given OS text scale.

commands.name: setEmulatedVisionDeficiency
commands.description: Emulates the given vision deficiency.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Vision deficiency to emulate. Order: best-effort emulations come first, followed by any
physiologically accurate emulations for medically recognized color vision deficiencies.

commands.name: setFocusEmulationEnabled
commands.description: Enables or disables simulating a focused and active page.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether to enable to disable focus emulation.

commands.name: setGeolocationOverride
commands.description:
Overrides the Geolocation Position or Error. Omitting latitude, longitude or
accuracy emulates position unavailable.

parameters.name: latitude
parameters.description: Mock latitude

parameters.name: longitude
parameters.description: Mock longitude

parameters.name: accuracy
parameters.description: Mock accuracy

parameters.name: altitude
parameters.description: Mock altitude

parameters.name: altitudeAccuracy
parameters.description: Mock altitudeAccuracy

parameters.name: heading
parameters.description: Mock heading

parameters.name: speed
parameters.description: Mock speed

parameters.name: hardwareConcurrency
parameters.description: Hardware concurrency to report

commands.name: setIdleOverride
commands.description: Overrides the Idle state.

parameters.name: isUserActive
parameters.description: Mock isUserActive

parameters.name: isScreenUnlocked
parameters.description: Mock isScreenUnlocked

commands.name: setLocaleOverride
commands.description: Overrides default host system locale with the specified one.

parameters.name: locale
parameters.description:
ICU style C locale (e.g. "en_US"). If not specified or empty, disables the override and
restores default host system locale.

commands.name: setNavigatorOverrides
commands.description: Overrides value returned by the javascript navigator object.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: The platform navigator.platform should return.

commands.name: setPageScaleFactor
commands.description: Sets a specified page scale factor.

parameters.name: pageScaleFactor
parameters.description: Page scale factor.

commands.name: setPressureDataOverride
commands.description:
Provides a given pressure data set that will be processed and eventually be
delivered to PressureObserver users. |source| must have been previously
overridden by setPressureSourceOverrideEnabled.

commands.name: setPressureSourceOverrideEnabled
commands.description:
Overrides a pressure source of a given type, as used by the Compute
Pressure API, so that updates to PressureObserver.observe() are provided
via setPressureStateOverride instead of being retrieved from
platform-provided telemetry data.

commands.name: setPressureStateOverride
commands.description:
TODO: OBSOLETE: To remove when setPressureDataOverride is merged.
Provides a given pressure state that will be processed and eventually be
delivered to PressureObserver users. |source| must have been previously
overridden by setPressureSourceOverrideEnabled.

commands.name: setPrimaryScreen
commands.description:
Set primary screen. Only supported in headless mode.
Note that this changes the coordinate system origin to the top-left
of the new primary screen, updating the bounds and work areas
of all existing screens accordingly.

commands.name: setSafeAreaInsetsOverride
commands.description:
Overrides the values for env(safe-area-inset-*) and env(safe-area-max-inset-*). Unset values will cause the
respective variables to be undefined, even if previously overridden.

commands.name: setScriptExecutionDisabled
commands.description: Switches script execution in the page.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Whether script execution should be disabled in the page.

parameters.name: hidden
parameters.description: Whether scrollbars should be always hidden.

commands.name: setSensorOverrideEnabled
commands.description:
Overrides a platform sensor of a given type. If |enabled| is true, calls to
Sensor.start() will use a virtual sensor as backend rather than fetching
data from a real hardware sensor. Otherwise, existing virtual
sensor-backend Sensor objects will fire an error event and new calls to
Sensor.start() will attempt to use a real sensor instead.

commands.name: setSensorOverrideReadings
commands.description:
Updates the sensor readings reported by a sensor type previously overridden
by setSensorOverrideEnabled.

commands.name: setSmallViewportHeightDifferenceOverride
commands.description:
Allows overriding the difference between the small and large viewport sizes, which determine the
value of the `svh` and `lvh` unit, respectively. Only supported for top-level frames.

parameters.name: difference
parameters.description:
This will cause an element of size 100svh to be `difference` pixels smaller than an element
of size 100lvh.

commands.name: setTimezoneOverride
commands.description: Overrides default host system timezone with the specified one.

parameters.name: timezoneId
parameters.description:
The timezone identifier. List of supported timezones:
https://source.chromium.org/chromium/chromium/deps/icu.git/+/faee8bc70570192d82d2978a71e2a615788597d1:source/data/misc/metaZones.txt
If empty, disables the override and restores default host system timezone.

commands.name: setTouchEmulationEnabled
commands.description: Enables touch on platforms which do not support them.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether the touch event emulation should be enabled.

parameters.name: maxTouchPoints
parameters.description: Maximum touch points supported. Defaults to one.

commands.name: setUserAgentOverride
commands.description:
Allows overriding user agent with the given string.
`userAgentMetadata` must be set for Client Hint headers to be sent.

parameters.name: userAgent
parameters.description: User agent to use.

parameters.name: acceptLanguage
parameters.description: Browser language to emulate.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: The platform navigator.platform should return.

parameters.name: userAgentMetadata
parameters.description: To be sent in Sec-CH-UA-* headers and returned in navigator.userAgentData

commands.name: setVirtualTimePolicy
commands.description:
Turns on virtual time for all frames (replacing real-time with a synthetic time source) and sets
the current virtual time policy.  Note this supersedes any previous time budget.

parameters.name: budget
parameters.description:
If set, after this many virtual milliseconds have elapsed virtual time will be paused and a
virtualTimeBudgetExpired event is sent.

parameters.name: maxVirtualTimeTaskStarvationCount
parameters.description:
If set this specifies the maximum number of tasks that can be run before virtual is forced
forwards to prevent deadlock.

parameters.name: initialVirtualTime
parameters.description: If set, base::Time::Now will be overridden to initially return this value.

returns.name: virtualTimeTicksBase
returns.description: Absolute timestamp at which virtual time was first enabled (up time in milliseconds).

commands.name: setVisibleSize
commands.description:
Resizes the frame/viewport of the page. Note that this does not affect the frame's container
(e.g. browser window). Can be used to produce screenshots of the specified size. Not supported
on Android.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: Frame width (DIP).

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: Frame height (DIP).

commands.name: updateScreen
commands.description: Updates specified screen parameters. Only supported in headless mode.

parameters.name: screenId
parameters.description: Target screen identifier.

parameters.name: left
parameters.description: Offset of the left edge of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Offset of the top edge of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: The width of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: The height of the screen in pixels.

parameters.name: workAreaInsets
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's work area.

parameters.name: devicePixelRatio
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's device pixel ratio.

parameters.name: rotation
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's rotation angle. Available values are 0, 90, 180 and 270.

parameters.name: colorDepth
parameters.description: Specifies the screen's color depth in bits.

parameters.name: label
parameters.description: Specifies the descriptive label for the screen.

parameters.name: isInternal
parameters.description: Indicates whether the screen is internal to the device or external, attached to the device. Default is false.

events.name: screenOrientationLockChanged
events.description:
Fired when a page calls screen.orientation.lock() or screen.orientation.unlock()
while device emulation is enabled. This allows the DevTools frontend to update the
emulated device orientation accordingly.

parameters.name: locked
parameters.description: Whether the screen orientation is currently locked.

parameters.name: orientation
parameters.description: The orientation lock type requested by the page. Only set when locked is true.

events.name: virtualTimeBudgetExpired
events.description: Notification sent after the virtual time budget for the current VirtualTimePolicy has run out.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Current posture of the device

types.name: Emulation.DisabledImageType
types.description: Enum of image types that can be disabled.

properties.name: orientation
properties.description: Orientation of a display feature in relation to screen

properties.name: offset
properties.description:
The offset from the screen origin in either the x (for vertical
orientation) or y (for horizontal orientation) direction.

properties.name: maskLength
properties.description:
A display feature may mask content such that it is not physically
displayed - this length along with the offset describes this area.
A display feature that only splits content will have a 0 mask_length.

properties.name: top
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-inset-top.

properties.name: topMax
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-max-inset-top.

properties.name: left
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-inset-left.

properties.name: leftMax
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-max-inset-left.

properties.name: bottom
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-inset-bottom.

properties.name: bottomMax
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-max-inset-bottom.

properties.name: right
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-inset-right.

properties.name: rightMax
properties.description: Overrides safe-area-max-inset-right.

types.name: Emulation.ScreenInfo
types.description:
Screen information similar to the one returned by window.getScreenDetails() method,
see https://w3c.github.io/window-management/#screendetailed.

properties.name: left
properties.description: Offset of the left edge of the screen.

properties.name: top
properties.description: Offset of the top edge of the screen.

properties.name: width
properties.description: Width of the screen.

properties.name: height
properties.description: Height of the screen.

properties.name: availLeft
properties.description: Offset of the left edge of the available screen area.

properties.name: availTop
properties.description: Offset of the top edge of the available screen area.

properties.name: availWidth
properties.description: Width of the available screen area.

properties.name: availHeight
properties.description: Height of the available screen area.

properties.name: devicePixelRatio
properties.description: Specifies the screen's device pixel ratio.

properties.name: orientation
properties.description: Specifies the screen's orientation.

properties.name: colorDepth
properties.description: Specifies the screen's color depth in bits.

properties.name: isExtended
properties.description: Indicates whether the device has multiple screens.

properties.name: isInternal
properties.description: Indicates whether the screen is internal to the device or external, attached to the device.

properties.name: isPrimary
properties.description: Indicates whether the screen is set as the the operating system primary screen.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Specifies the descriptive label for the screen.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Specifies the unique identifier of the screen.

types.name: Emulation.ScreenOrientation
types.description: Screen orientation.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Orientation type.

properties.name: angle
properties.description: Orientation angle.

types.name: Emulation.SensorType
types.description:
Used to specify sensor types to emulate.
See https://w3c.github.io/sensors/#automation for more information.

types.name: Emulation.UserAgentBrandVersion
types.description: Used to specify User Agent Client Hints to emulate. See https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints

types.name: Emulation.UserAgentMetadata
types.description:
Used to specify User Agent Client Hints to emulate. See https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints
Missing optional values will be filled in by the target with what it would normally use.

properties.name: brands
properties.description: Brands appearing in Sec-CH-UA.

properties.name: fullVersionList
properties.description: Brands appearing in Sec-CH-UA-Full-Version-List.

properties.name: formFactors
properties.description:
Used to specify User Agent form-factor values.
See https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints/#sec-ch-ua-form-factors

types.name: Emulation.VirtualTimePolicy
types.description:
advance: If the scheduler runs out of immediate work, the virtual time base may fast forward to
allow the next delayed task (if any) to run; pause: The virtual time base may not advance;
pauseIfNetworkFetchesPending: The virtual time base may not advance if there are any pending
resource fetches.

properties.name: top

domains.name: EventBreakpoints
domains.description:
EventBreakpoints permits setting JavaScript breakpoints on operations and events
occurring in native code invoked from JavaScript. Once breakpoint is hit, it is
reported through Debugger domain, similarly to regular breakpoints being hit.

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Removes all breakpoints

commands.name: removeInstrumentationBreakpoint
commands.description: Removes breakpoint on particular native event.

parameters.name: eventName
parameters.description: Instrumentation name to stop on.

commands.name: setInstrumentationBreakpoint
commands.description: Sets breakpoint on particular native event.

parameters.name: eventName
parameters.description: Instrumentation name to stop on.

domains.name: Extensions
domains.description: Defines commands and events for browser extensions.

commands.name: clearStorageItems
commands.description: Clears extension storage in the given `storageArea`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of extension.

parameters.name: storageArea
parameters.description: StorageArea to remove data from.

commands.name: getExtensions
commands.description:
Gets a list of all unpacked extensions.
Available if the client is connected using the --remote-debugging-pipe flag
and the --enable-unsafe-extension-debugging flag is set.

commands.name: getStorageItems
commands.description:
Gets data from extension storage in the given `storageArea`. If `keys` is
specified, these are used to filter the result.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of extension.

parameters.name: storageArea
parameters.description: StorageArea to retrieve data from.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Keys to retrieve.

commands.name: loadUnpacked
commands.description:
Installs an unpacked extension from the filesystem similar to
--load-extension CLI flags. Returns extension ID once the extension
has been installed. Available if the client is connected using the
--remote-debugging-pipe flag and the --enable-unsafe-extension-debugging
flag is set.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Absolute file path.

parameters.name: enableInIncognito
parameters.description: Enable the extension in incognito

returns.name: id
returns.description: Extension id.

commands.name: removeStorageItems
commands.description: Removes `keys` from extension storage in the given `storageArea`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of extension.

parameters.name: storageArea
parameters.description: StorageArea to remove data from.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Keys to remove.

commands.name: setStorageItems
commands.description:
Sets `values` in extension storage in the given `storageArea`. The provided `values`
will be merged with existing values in the storage area.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of extension.

parameters.name: storageArea
parameters.description: StorageArea to set data in.

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: Values to set.

commands.name: triggerAction
commands.description:
Runs an extension default action.
Available if the client is connected using the --remote-debugging-pipe
flag and the --enable-unsafe-extension-debugging flag is set.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Extension id.

parameters.name: targetId
parameters.description: A tab target ID to trigger the default extension action on.

commands.name: uninstall
commands.description:
Uninstalls an unpacked extension (others not supported) from the profile.
Available if the client is connected using the --remote-debugging-pipe flag
and the --enable-unsafe-extension-debugging.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Extension id.
