domains.name: FedCm
domains.description: This domain allows interacting with the FedCM dialog.

parameters.name: disableRejectionDelay
parameters.description:
Allows callers to disable the promise rejection delay that would
normally happen, if this is unimportant to what's being tested.
(step 4 of https://fedidcg.github.io/FedCM/#browser-api-rp-sign-in)

commands.name: resetCooldown
commands.description:
Resets the cooldown time, if any, to allow the next FedCM call to show
a dialog even if one was recently dismissed by the user.

events.name: dialogClosed
events.description:
Triggered when a dialog is closed, either by user action, JS abort,
or a command below.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description:
These exist primarily so that the caller can verify the
RP context was used appropriately.

types.name: FedCm.Account
types.description: Corresponds to IdentityRequestAccount

properties.name: termsOfServiceUrl
properties.description: These two are only set if the loginState is signUp

types.name: FedCm.AccountUrlType
types.description: The URLs that each account has

types.name: FedCm.DialogButton
types.description: The buttons on the FedCM dialog.

types.name: FedCm.DialogType
types.description: The types of FedCM dialogs.

types.name: FedCm.LoginState
types.description:
Whether this is a sign-up or sign-in action for this account, i.e.
whether this account has ever been used to sign in to this RP before.

domains.name: Fetch
domains.description: A domain for letting clients substitute browser's network layer with client code.

commands.name: continueRequest
commands.description: Continues the request, optionally modifying some of its parameters.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: An id the client received in requestPaused event.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: If set, the request url will be modified in a way that's not observable by page.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: If set, the request method is overridden.

parameters.name: postData
parameters.description: If set, overrides the post data in the request. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description:
If set, overrides the request headers. Note that the overrides do not
extend to subsequent redirect hops, if a redirect happens. Another override
may be applied to a different request produced by a redirect.

parameters.name: interceptResponse
parameters.description: If set, overrides response interception behavior for this request.

commands.name: continueResponse
commands.description:
Continues loading of the paused response, optionally modifying the
response headers. If either responseCode or headers are modified, all of them
must be present.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: An id the client received in requestPaused event.

parameters.name: responseCode
parameters.description: An HTTP response code. If absent, original response code will be used.

parameters.name: responsePhrase
parameters.description:
A textual representation of responseCode.
If absent, a standard phrase matching responseCode is used.

parameters.name: responseHeaders
parameters.description: Response headers. If absent, original response headers will be used.

parameters.name: binaryResponseHeaders
parameters.description:
Alternative way of specifying response headers as a \0-separated
series of name: value pairs. Prefer the above method unless you
need to represent some non-UTF8 values that can't be transmitted
over the protocol as text. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

commands.name: continueWithAuth
commands.description: Continues a request supplying authChallengeResponse following authRequired event.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: An id the client received in authRequired event.

parameters.name: authChallengeResponse
parameters.description: Response to  with an authChallenge.

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables the fetch domain.

commands.name: enable
commands.description:
Enables issuing of requestPaused events. A request will be paused until client
calls one of failRequest, fulfillRequest or continueRequest/continueWithAuth.

parameters.name: patterns
parameters.description:
If specified, only requests matching any of these patterns will produce
fetchRequested event and will be paused until clients response. If not set,
all requests will be affected.

parameters.name: handleAuthRequests
parameters.description:
If true, authRequired events will be issued and requests will be paused
expecting a call to continueWithAuth.

commands.name: failRequest
commands.description: Causes the request to fail with specified reason.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: An id the client received in requestPaused event.

parameters.name: errorReason
parameters.description: Causes the request to fail with the given reason.

commands.name: fulfillRequest
commands.description: Provides response to the request.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: An id the client received in requestPaused event.

parameters.name: responseCode
parameters.description: An HTTP response code.

parameters.name: responseHeaders
parameters.description: Response headers.

parameters.name: binaryResponseHeaders
parameters.description:
Alternative way of specifying response headers as a \0-separated
series of name: value pairs. Prefer the above method unless you
need to represent some non-UTF8 values that can't be transmitted
over the protocol as text. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
A response body. If absent, original response body will be used if
the request is intercepted at the response stage and empty body
will be used if the request is intercepted at the request stage. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

parameters.name: responsePhrase
parameters.description:
A textual representation of responseCode.
If absent, a standard phrase matching responseCode is used.

commands.name: getResponseBody
commands.description:
Causes the body of the response to be received from the server and
returned as a single string. May only be issued for a request that
is paused in the Response stage and is mutually exclusive with
takeResponseBodyForInterceptionAsStream. Calling other methods that
affect the request or disabling fetch domain before body is received
results in an undefined behavior.
Note that the response body is not available for redirects. Requests
paused in the _redirect received_ state may be differentiated by
`responseCode` and presence of `location` response header, see
comments to `requestPaused` for details.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Identifier for the intercepted request to get body for.

returns.name: body
returns.description: Response body.

returns.name: base64Encoded
returns.description: True, if content was sent as base64.

commands.name: takeResponseBodyAsStream
commands.description:
Returns a handle to the stream representing the response body.
The request must be paused in the HeadersReceived stage.
Note that after this command the request can't be continued
as is -- client either needs to cancel it or to provide the
response body.
The stream only supports sequential read, IO.read will fail if the position
is specified.
This method is mutually exclusive with getResponseBody.
Calling other methods that affect the request or disabling fetch
domain before body is received results in an undefined behavior.

events.name: authRequired
events.description:
Issued when the domain is enabled with handleAuthRequests set to true.
The request is paused until client responds with continueWithAuth.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Each request the page makes will have a unique id.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: The details of the request.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: The id of the frame that initiated the request.

parameters.name: resourceType
parameters.description: How the requested resource will be used.

parameters.name: authChallenge
parameters.description:
Details of the Authorization Challenge encountered.
If this is set, client should respond with continueRequest that
contains AuthChallengeResponse.

events.name: requestPaused
events.description:
Issued when the domain is enabled and the request URL matches the
specified filter. The request is paused until the client responds
with one of continueRequest, failRequest or fulfillRequest.
The stage of the request can be determined by presence of responseErrorReason
and responseStatusCode -- the request is at the response stage if either
of these fields is present and in the request stage otherwise.
Redirect responses and subsequent requests are reported similarly to regular
responses and requests. Redirect responses may be distinguished by the value
of `responseStatusCode` (which is one of 301, 302, 303, 307, 308) along with
presence of the `location` header. Requests resulting from a redirect will
have `redirectedRequestId` field set.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Each request the page makes will have a unique id.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: The details of the request.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: The id of the frame that initiated the request.

parameters.name: resourceType
parameters.description: How the requested resource will be used.

parameters.name: responseErrorReason
parameters.description: Response error if intercepted at response stage.

parameters.name: responseStatusCode
parameters.description: Response code if intercepted at response stage.

parameters.name: responseStatusText
parameters.description: Response status text if intercepted at response stage.

parameters.name: responseHeaders
parameters.description: Response headers if intercepted at the response stage.

parameters.name: networkId
parameters.description:
If the intercepted request had a corresponding Network.requestWillBeSent event fired for it,
then this networkId will be the same as the requestId present in the requestWillBeSent event.

parameters.name: redirectedRequestId
parameters.description:
If the request is due to a redirect response from the server, the id of the request that
has caused the redirect.

types.name: Fetch.AuthChallenge
types.description: Authorization challenge for HTTP status code 401 or 407.

properties.name: source
properties.description: Source of the authentication challenge.

properties.name: origin
properties.description: Origin of the challenger.

properties.name: scheme
properties.description: The authentication scheme used, such as basic or digest

properties.name: realm
properties.description: The realm of the challenge. May be empty.

types.name: Fetch.AuthChallengeResponse
types.description: Response to an AuthChallenge.

properties.name: response
properties.description:
The decision on what to do in response to the authorization challenge.  Default means
deferring to the default behavior of the net stack, which will likely either the Cancel
authentication or display a popup dialog box.

properties.name: username
properties.description:
The username to provide, possibly empty. Should only be set if response is
ProvideCredentials.

properties.name: password
properties.description:
The password to provide, possibly empty. Should only be set if response is
ProvideCredentials.

types.name: Fetch.HeaderEntry
types.description: Response HTTP header entry

types.name: Fetch.RequestId
types.description:
Unique request identifier.
Note that this does not identify individual HTTP requests that are part of
a network request.

properties.name: urlPattern
properties.description:
Wildcards (`'*'` -> zero or more, `'?'` -> exactly one) are allowed. Escape character is
backslash. Omitting is equivalent to `"*"`.

properties.name: resourceType
properties.description: If set, only requests for matching resource types will be intercepted.

properties.name: requestStage
properties.description: Stage at which to begin intercepting requests. Default is Request.

types.name: Fetch.RequestStage
types.description:
Stages of the request to handle. Request will intercept before the request is
sent. Response will intercept after the response is received (but before response
body is received).

returns.name: directory
returns.description: Returns the directory object at the path.

properties.name: storageKey
properties.description: Storage key

properties.name: bucketName
properties.description: Bucket name. Not passing a `bucketName` will retrieve the default Bucket. (https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Storage_API#storage_buckets)

properties.name: pathComponents
properties.description: Path to the directory using each path component as an array item.

properties.name: nestedFiles
properties.description: Files that are directly nested under this directory.

properties.name: lastModified
properties.description: Timestamp

properties.name: size
properties.description: Size in bytes

domains.name: HeadlessExperimental
domains.description: This domain provides experimental commands only supported in headless mode.

commands.name: beginFrame
commands.description:
Sends a BeginFrame to the target and returns when the frame was completed. Optionally captures a
screenshot from the resulting frame. Requires that the target was created with enabled
BeginFrameControl. Designed for use with --run-all-compositor-stages-before-draw, see also
https://goo.gle/chrome-headless-rendering for more background.

parameters.name: frameTimeTicks
parameters.description:
Timestamp of this BeginFrame in Renderer TimeTicks (milliseconds of uptime). If not set,
the current time will be used.

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description:
The interval between BeginFrames that is reported to the compositor, in milliseconds.
Defaults to a 60 frames/second interval, i.e. about 16.666 milliseconds.

parameters.name: noDisplayUpdates
parameters.description:
Whether updates should not be committed and drawn onto the display. False by default. If
true, only side effects of the BeginFrame will be run, such as layout and animations, but
any visual updates may not be visible on the display or in screenshots.

parameters.name: screenshot
parameters.description:
If set, a screenshot of the frame will be captured and returned in the response. Otherwise,
no screenshot will be captured. Note that capturing a screenshot can fail, for example,
during renderer initialization. In such a case, no screenshot data will be returned.

returns.name: hasDamage
returns.description:
Whether the BeginFrame resulted in damage and, thus, a new frame was committed to the
display. Reported for diagnostic uses, may be removed in the future.

returns.name: screenshotData
returns.description: Base64-encoded image data of the screenshot, if one was requested and successfully taken. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables headless events for the target.

commands.name: enable
commands.description: Enables headless events for the target.

types.name: HeadlessExperimental.ScreenshotParams
types.description: Encoding options for a screenshot.

properties.name: format
properties.description: Image compression format (defaults to png).

properties.name: quality
properties.description: Compression quality from range [0..100] (jpeg and webp only).

properties.name: optimizeForSpeed
properties.description: Optimize image encoding for speed, not for resulting size (defaults to false)

commands.name: addInspectedHeapObject
commands.description:
Enables console to refer to the node with given id via $x (see Command Line API for more details
$x functions).

parameters.name: heapObjectId
parameters.description: Heap snapshot object id to be accessible by means of $x command line API.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: Identifier of the object to get heap object id for.

returns.name: heapSnapshotObjectId
returns.description: Id of the heap snapshot object corresponding to the passed remote object id.

parameters.name: objectGroup
parameters.description: Symbolic group name that can be used to release multiple objects.

returns.name: result
returns.description: Evaluation result.

returns.name: profile
returns.description: Return the sampling profile being collected.

parameters.name: samplingInterval
parameters.description:
Average sample interval in bytes. Poisson distribution is used for the intervals. The
default value is 32768 bytes.

parameters.name: stackDepth
parameters.description: Maximum stack depth. The default value is 128.

parameters.name: includeObjectsCollectedByMajorGC
parameters.description:
By default, the sampling heap profiler reports only objects which are
still alive when the profile is returned via getSamplingProfile or
stopSampling, which is useful for determining what functions contribute
the most to steady-state memory usage. This flag instructs the sampling
heap profiler to also include information about objects discarded by
major GC, which will show which functions cause large temporary memory
usage or long GC pauses.

parameters.name: includeObjectsCollectedByMinorGC
parameters.description:
By default, the sampling heap profiler reports only objects which are
still alive when the profile is returned via getSamplingProfile or
stopSampling, which is useful for determining what functions contribute
the most to steady-state memory usage. This flag instructs the sampling
heap profiler to also include information about objects discarded by
minor GC, which is useful when tuning a latency-sensitive application
for minimal GC activity.

returns.name: profile
returns.description: Recorded sampling heap profile.

parameters.name: reportProgress
parameters.description:
If true 'reportHeapSnapshotProgress' events will be generated while snapshot is being taken
when the tracking is stopped.

parameters.name: treatGlobalObjectsAsRoots
parameters.description: Deprecated in favor of `exposeInternals`.

parameters.name: captureNumericValue
parameters.description: If true, numerical values are included in the snapshot

parameters.name: exposeInternals
parameters.description: If true, exposes internals of the snapshot.

parameters.name: reportProgress
parameters.description: If true 'reportHeapSnapshotProgress' events will be generated while snapshot is being taken.

parameters.name: treatGlobalObjectsAsRoots
parameters.description:
If true, a raw snapshot without artificial roots will be generated.
Deprecated in favor of `exposeInternals`.

parameters.name: captureNumericValue
parameters.description: If true, numerical values are included in the snapshot

parameters.name: exposeInternals
parameters.description: If true, exposes internals of the snapshot.

events.name: heapStatsUpdate
events.description: If heap objects tracking has been started then backend may send update for one or more fragments

parameters.name: statsUpdate
parameters.description:
An array of triplets. Each triplet describes a fragment. The first integer is the fragment
index, the second integer is a total count of objects for the fragment, the third integer is
a total size of the objects for the fragment.

events.name: lastSeenObjectId
events.description:
If heap objects tracking has been started then backend regularly sends a current value for last
seen object id and corresponding timestamp. If the were changes in the heap since last event
then one or more heapStatsUpdate events will be sent before a new lastSeenObjectId event.

properties.description: Index key path.

properties.name: unique
properties.description: If true, index is unique.

properties.name: multiEntry
properties.description: If true, index allows multiple entries for a key.

commands.name: cancelDragging
commands.description: Cancels any active dragging in the page.

commands.name: dispatchDragEvent
commands.description: Dispatches a drag event into the page.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of the drag event.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: X coordinate of the event relative to the main frame's viewport in CSS pixels.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description:
Y coordinate of the event relative to the main frame's viewport in CSS pixels. 0 refers to
the top of the viewport and Y increases as it proceeds towards the bottom of the viewport.

parameters.name: modifiers
parameters.description:
Bit field representing pressed modifier keys. Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta/Command=4, Shift=8
(default: 0).

commands.name: dispatchKeyEvent
commands.description: Dispatches a key event to the page.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of the key event.

parameters.name: modifiers
parameters.description:
Bit field representing pressed modifier keys. Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta/Command=4, Shift=8
(default: 0).

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Time at which the event occurred.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description:
Text as generated by processing a virtual key code with a keyboard layout. Not needed for
for `keyUp` and `rawKeyDown` events (default: "")

parameters.name: unmodifiedText
parameters.description:
Text that would have been generated by the keyboard if no modifiers were pressed (except for
shift). Useful for shortcut (accelerator) key handling (default: "").

parameters.name: keyIdentifier
parameters.description: Unique key identifier (e.g., 'U+0041') (default: "").

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Unique DOM defined string value for each physical key (e.g., 'KeyA') (default: "").

parameters.name: key
parameters.description:
Unique DOM defined string value describing the meaning of the key in the context of active
modifiers, keyboard layout, etc (e.g., 'AltGr') (default: "").

parameters.name: windowsVirtualKeyCode
parameters.description: Windows virtual key code (default: 0).

parameters.name: nativeVirtualKeyCode
parameters.description: Native virtual key code (default: 0).

parameters.name: autoRepeat
parameters.description: Whether the event was generated from auto repeat (default: false).

parameters.name: isKeypad
parameters.description: Whether the event was generated from the keypad (default: false).

parameters.name: isSystemKey
parameters.description: Whether the event was a system key event (default: false).

parameters.name: location
parameters.description:
Whether the event was from the left or right side of the keyboard. 1=Left, 2=Right (default:
0).

parameters.name: commands
parameters.description:
Editing commands to send with the key event (e.g., 'selectAll') (default: []).
These are related to but not equal the command names used in `document.execCommand` and NSStandardKeyBindingResponding.
See https://source.chromium.org/chromium/chromium/src/+/main:third_party/blink/renderer/core/editing/commands/editor_command_names.h for valid command names.

commands.name: dispatchMouseEvent
commands.description: Dispatches a mouse event to the page.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of the mouse event.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: X coordinate of the event relative to the main frame's viewport in CSS pixels.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description:
Y coordinate of the event relative to the main frame's viewport in CSS pixels. 0 refers to
the top of the viewport and Y increases as it proceeds towards the bottom of the viewport.

parameters.name: modifiers
parameters.description:
Bit field representing pressed modifier keys. Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta/Command=4, Shift=8
(default: 0).

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Time at which the event occurred.

parameters.name: button
parameters.description: Mouse button (default: "none").

parameters.name: buttons
parameters.description:
A number indicating which buttons are pressed on the mouse when a mouse event is triggered.
Left=1, Right=2, Middle=4, Back=8, Forward=16, None=0.

parameters.name: clickCount
parameters.description: Number of times the mouse button was clicked (default: 0).

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: The normalized pressure, which has a range of [0,1] (default: 0).

parameters.name: tangentialPressure
parameters.description: The normalized tangential pressure, which has a range of [-1,1] (default: 0).

parameters.name: tiltX
parameters.description: The plane angle between the Y-Z plane and the plane containing both the stylus axis and the Y axis, in degrees of the range [-90,90], a positive tiltX is to the right (default: 0).

parameters.name: tiltY
parameters.description: The plane angle between the X-Z plane and the plane containing both the stylus axis and the X axis, in degrees of the range [-90,90], a positive tiltY is towards the user (default: 0).

parameters.name: twist
parameters.description: The clockwise rotation of a pen stylus around its own major axis, in degrees in the range [0,359] (default: 0).

parameters.name: deltaX
parameters.description: X delta in CSS pixels for mouse wheel event (default: 0).

parameters.name: deltaY
parameters.description: Y delta in CSS pixels for mouse wheel event (default: 0).

parameters.name: pointerType
parameters.description: Pointer type (default: "mouse").

commands.name: dispatchTouchEvent
commands.description: Dispatches a touch event to the page.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
Type of the touch event. TouchEnd and TouchCancel must not contain any touch points, while
TouchStart and TouchMove must contains at least one.

parameters.name: touchPoints
parameters.description:
Active touch points on the touch device. One event per any changed point (compared to
previous touch event in a sequence) is generated, emulating pressing/moving/releasing points
one by one.

parameters.name: modifiers
parameters.description:
Bit field representing pressed modifier keys. Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta/Command=4, Shift=8
(default: 0).

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Time at which the event occurred.

commands.name: emulateTouchFromMouseEvent
commands.description: Emulates touch event from the mouse event parameters.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of the mouse event.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: X coordinate of the mouse pointer in DIP.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Y coordinate of the mouse pointer in DIP.

parameters.name: button
parameters.description: Mouse button. Only "none", "left", "right" are supported.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Time at which the event occurred (default: current time).

parameters.name: deltaX
parameters.description: X delta in DIP for mouse wheel event (default: 0).

parameters.name: deltaY
parameters.description: Y delta in DIP for mouse wheel event (default: 0).

parameters.name: modifiers
parameters.description:
Bit field representing pressed modifier keys. Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta/Command=4, Shift=8
(default: 0).

parameters.name: clickCount
parameters.description: Number of times the mouse button was clicked (default: 0).

commands.name: imeSetComposition

This method sets the current candidate text for IME.
Use imeCommitComposition to commit the final text.
Use imeSetComposition with empty string as text to cancel composition.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text to insert

parameters.name: selectionStart
parameters.description: selection start

parameters.name: selectionEnd
parameters.description: selection end

parameters.name: replacementStart
parameters.description: replacement start

parameters.name: replacementEnd

This method emulates inserting text that doesn't come from a key press,
for example an emoji keyboard or an IME.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text to insert.

commands.name: setIgnoreInputEvents
commands.description: Ignores input events (useful while auditing page).

parameters.name: ignore
parameters.description: Ignores input events processing when set to true.

commands.name: setInterceptDrags
commands.description:
Prevents default drag and drop behavior and instead emits `Input.dragIntercepted` events.
Drag and drop behavior can be directly controlled via `Input.dispatchDragEvent`.

commands.name: synthesizePinchGesture
commands.description: Synthesizes a pinch gesture over a time period by issuing appropriate touch events.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: X coordinate of the start of the gesture in CSS pixels.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Y coordinate of the start of the gesture in CSS pixels.

parameters.name: scaleFactor
parameters.description: Relative scale factor after zooming (>1.0 zooms in, <1.0 zooms out).

parameters.name: relativeSpeed
parameters.description: Relative pointer speed in pixels per second (default: 800).

parameters.name: gestureSourceType
parameters.description:
Which type of input events to be generated (default: 'default', which queries the platform
for the preferred input type).

commands.name: synthesizeScrollGesture
commands.description: Synthesizes a scroll gesture over a time period by issuing appropriate touch events.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: X coordinate of the start of the gesture in CSS pixels.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Y coordinate of the start of the gesture in CSS pixels.

parameters.name: xDistance
parameters.description: The distance to scroll along the X axis (positive to scroll left).

parameters.name: yDistance
parameters.description: The distance to scroll along the Y axis (positive to scroll up).

parameters.name: xOverscroll
parameters.description:
The number of additional pixels to scroll back along the X axis, in addition to the given
distance.

parameters.name: yOverscroll
parameters.description:
The number of additional pixels to scroll back along the Y axis, in addition to the given
distance.

parameters.name: preventFling
parameters.description: Prevent fling (default: true).

parameters.name: speed
parameters.description: Swipe speed in pixels per second (default: 800).

parameters.name: gestureSourceType
parameters.description:
Which type of input events to be generated (default: 'default', which queries the platform
for the preferred input type).

parameters.name: repeatCount
parameters.description: The number of times to repeat the gesture (default: 0).

parameters.name: repeatDelayMs
parameters.description: The number of milliseconds delay between each repeat. (default: 250).

parameters.name: interactionMarkerName
parameters.description: The name of the interaction markers to generate, if not empty (default: "").

commands.name: synthesizeTapGesture
commands.description: Synthesizes a tap gesture over a time period by issuing appropriate touch events.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: X coordinate of the start of the gesture in CSS pixels.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Y coordinate of the start of the gesture in CSS pixels.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Duration between touchdown and touchup events in ms (default: 50).

parameters.name: tapCount
parameters.description: Number of times to perform the tap (e.g. 2 for double tap, default: 1).

parameters.name: gestureSourceType
parameters.description:
Which type of input events to be generated (default: 'default', which queries the platform
for the preferred input type).

events.name: dragIntercepted
events.description:
Emitted only when `Input.setInterceptDrags` is enabled. Use this data with `Input.dispatchDragEvent` to
restore normal drag and drop behavior.

properties.name: baseURL

Stores the base URL for the contained markup. Only valid when `mimeType`
== "text/html".

types.name: Input.TimeSinceEpoch

properties.name: x

properties.name: tangentialPressure
properties.description: The normalized tangential pressure, which has a range of [-1,1] (default: 0).

properties.name: tiltX
properties.description: The plane angle between the Y-Z plane and the plane containing both the stylus axis and the Y axis, in degrees of the range [-90,90], a positive tiltX is to the right (default: 0)

properties.name: tiltY
properties.description: The plane angle between the X-Z plane and the plane containing both the stylus axis and the X axis, in degrees of the range [-90,90], a positive tiltY is towards the user (default: 0).

properties.description: The clockwise rotation of a pen stylus around its own major axis, in degrees in the range [0,359] (default: 0).

domains.description: This domain allows detailed inspection of media elements.

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables the Media domain.

commands.description: Enables the Media domain

events.name: playerCreated
events.description:
Called whenever a player is created, or when a new agent joins and receives
a list of active players. If an agent is restored, it will receive one
event for each active player.

events.name: playerErrorsRaised
events.description: Send a list of any errors that need to be delivered.

events.name: playerEventsAdded
events.description:
Send events as a list, allowing them to be batched on the browser for less
congestion. If batched, events must ALWAYS be in chronological order.

events.name: playerMessagesLogged
events.description: Send a list of any messages that need to be delivered.

events.name: playerPropertiesChanged
events.description:
This can be called multiple times, and can be used to set / override /
remove player properties. A null propValue indicates removal.

types.name: Media.PlayerError
types.description: Corresponds to kMediaError

properties.name: code
properties.description:
Code is the numeric enum entry for a specific set of error codes, such
as PipelineStatusCodes in media/base/pipeline_status.h

properties.name: stack
properties.description: A trace of where this error was caused / where it passed through.

properties.name: cause
properties.description:
Errors potentially have a root cause error, ie, a DecoderError might be
caused by an WindowsError

properties.name: data
properties.description: Extra data attached to an error, such as an HRESULT, Video Codec, etc.

types.name: Media.PlayerErrorSourceLocation
types.description:
Represents logged source line numbers reported in an error.
NOTE: file and line are from chromium c++ implementation code, not js.

types.name: Media.PlayerEvent
types.description: Corresponds to kMediaEventTriggered

types.name: Media.PlayerId
types.description: Players will get an ID that is unique within the agent context.

types.name: Media.PlayerMessage
types.description:
Have one type per entry in MediaLogRecord::Type
Corresponds to kMessage

properties.name: level
properties.description:
Keep in sync with MediaLogMessageLevel
We are currently keeping the message level 'error' separate from the
PlayerError type because right now they represent different things,
this one being a DVLOG(ERROR) style log message that gets printed
based on what log level is selected in the UI, and the other is a
representation of a media::PipelineStatus object. Soon however we're
going to be moving away from using PipelineStatus for errors and
introducing a new error type which should hopefully let us integrate
the error log level into the PlayerError type.

types.name: Media.PlayerProperty
types.description: Corresponds to kMediaPropertyChange

If maxTotalBufferSize is not set, durable messages are disabled.

parameters.description: Buffer size in bytes to use when preserving network payloads (XHRs, etc).

parameters.name: maxResourceBufferSize
parameters.description: Per-resource buffer size in bytes to use when preserving network payloads (XHRs, etc).

commands.name: continueInterceptedRequest
commands.description:
Response to Network.requestIntercepted which either modifies the request to continue with any
modifications, or blocks it, or completes it with the provided response bytes. If a network
fetch occurs as a result which encounters a redirect an additional Network.requestIntercepted
event will be sent with the same InterceptionId.
Deprecated, use Fetch.continueRequest, Fetch.fulfillRequest and Fetch.failRequest instead.

parameters.name: errorReason
parameters.description:
If set this causes the request to fail with the given reason. Passing `Aborted` for requests
marked with `isNavigationRequest` also cancels the navigation. Must not be set in response
to an authChallenge.

parameters.name: rawResponse
parameters.description:
If set the requests completes using with the provided base64 encoded raw response, including
HTTP status line and headers etc... Must not be set in response to an authChallenge. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

parameters.name: url
parameters.description:
If set the request url will be modified in a way that's not observable by page. Must not be
set in response to an authChallenge.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description:
If set this allows the request method to be overridden. Must not be set in response to an
authChallenge.

parameters.name: postData
parameters.description: If set this allows postData to be set. Must not be set in response to an authChallenge.

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description:
If set this allows the request headers to be changed. Must not be set in response to an
authChallenge.

parameters.name: authChallengeResponse
parameters.description: Response to a requestIntercepted with an authChallenge. Must not be set otherwise.

commands.name: deleteCookies
commands.description: Deletes browser cookies with matching name and url or domain/path/partitionKey pair.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of the cookies to remove.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description:
If specified, deletes all the cookies with the given name where domain and path match
provided URL.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: If specified, deletes only cookies with the exact domain.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: If specified, deletes only cookies with the exact path.

parameters.name: partitionKey
parameters.description:
If specified, deletes only cookies with the the given name and partitionKey where
all partition key attributes match the cookie partition key attribute.

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables network tracking, prevents network events from being sent to the client.

commands.name: emulateNetworkConditions
commands.description:
Activates emulation of network conditions. This command is deprecated in favor of the emulateNetworkConditionsByRule
and overrideNetworkState commands, which can be used together to the same effect.

parameters.name: offline
parameters.description: True to emulate internet disconnection.

parameters.name: latency
parameters.description: Minimum latency from request sent to response headers received (ms).

parameters.name: downloadThroughput
parameters.description: Maximal aggregated download throughput (bytes/sec). -1 disables download throttling.

parameters.name: uploadThroughput
parameters.description: Maximal aggregated upload throughput (bytes/sec).  -1 disables upload throttling.

parameters.name: connectionType
parameters.description: Connection type if known.

parameters.name: packetLoss
parameters.description: WebRTC packet loss (percent, 0-100). 0 disables packet loss emulation, 100 drops all the packets.

parameters.name: packetQueueLength
parameters.description: WebRTC packet queue length (packet). 0 removes any queue length limitations.

parameters.name: packetReordering
parameters.description: WebRTC packetReordering feature.

commands.name: emulateNetworkConditionsByRule
commands.description:
Activates emulation of network conditions for individual requests using URL match patterns. Unlike the deprecated
Network.emulateNetworkConditions this method does not affect `navigator` state. Use Network.overrideNetworkState to
explicitly modify `navigator` behavior.

parameters.name: offline
parameters.description: True to emulate internet disconnection.

parameters.name: matchedNetworkConditions
parameters.description:
Configure conditions for matching requests. If multiple entries match a request, the first entry wins.  Global
conditions can be configured by leaving the urlPattern for the conditions empty. These global conditions are
also applied for throttling of p2p connections.

returns.name: ruleIds
returns.description:
An id for each entry in matchedNetworkConditions. The id will be included in the requestWillBeSentExtraInfo for
requests affected by a rule.

commands.name: enable
commands.description: Enables network tracking, network events will now be delivered to the client.

parameters.name: maxTotalBufferSize
parameters.description:
Buffer size in bytes to use when preserving network payloads (XHRs, etc).
This is the maximum number of bytes that will be collected by this
DevTools session.

parameters.name: maxResourceBufferSize
parameters.description: Per-resource buffer size in bytes to use when preserving network payloads (XHRs, etc).

parameters.name: maxPostDataSize
parameters.description: Longest post body size (in bytes) that would be included in requestWillBeSent notification

parameters.name: reportDirectSocketTraffic
parameters.description: Whether DirectSocket chunk send/receive events should be reported.

parameters.name: enableDurableMessages
parameters.description:
Enable storing response bodies outside of renderer, so that these survive
a cross-process navigation. Requires maxTotalBufferSize to be set.
Currently defaults to false. This field is being deprecated in favor of the dedicated
configureDurableMessages command, due to the possibility of deadlocks when awaiting
Network.enable before issuing Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger.

commands.name: enableDeviceBoundSessions
commands.description: Sets up tracking device bound sessions and fetching of initial set of sessions.

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: Whether to enable or disable events.

commands.name: enableReportingApi
commands.description:
Enables tracking for the Reporting API, events generated by the Reporting API will now be delivered to the client.
Enabling triggers 'reportingApiReportAdded' for all existing reports.

parameters.name: enable
parameters.description: Whether to enable or disable events for the Reporting API

commands.name: fetchSchemefulSite
commands.description: Fetches the schemeful site for a specific origin.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: The URL origin.

returns.name: schemefulSite
returns.description: The corresponding schemeful site.

commands.name: getAllCookies
commands.description:
Returns all browser cookies. Depending on the backend support, will return detailed cookie
information in the `cookies` field.
Deprecated. Use Storage.getCookies instead.

returns.name: cookies
returns.description: Array of cookie objects.

commands.name: getCertificate
commands.description: Returns the DER-encoded certificate.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: Origin to get certificate for.

commands.name: getCookies
commands.description:
Returns all browser cookies for the current URL. Depending on the backend support, will return
detailed cookie information in the `cookies` field.

parameters.name: urls
parameters.description:
The list of URLs for which applicable cookies will be fetched.
If not specified, it's assumed to be set to the list containing
the URLs of the page and all of its subframes.

returns.name: cookies
returns.description: Array of cookie objects.

commands.name: getRequestPostData
commands.description: Returns post data sent with the request. Returns an error when no data was sent with the request.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Identifier of the network request to get content for.

returns.name: postData
returns.description: Request body string, omitting files from multipart requests

returns.name: base64Encoded
returns.description: True, if content was sent as base64.

commands.name: getResponseBody
commands.description: Returns content served for the given request.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Identifier of the network request to get content for.

returns.name: body
returns.description: Response body.

returns.name: base64Encoded
returns.description: True, if content was sent as base64.

commands.name: getResponseBodyForInterception
commands.description: Returns content served for the given currently intercepted request.

parameters.name: interceptionId
parameters.description: Identifier for the intercepted request to get body for.

returns.name: body
returns.description: Response body.

returns.name: base64Encoded
returns.description: True, if content was sent as base64.

commands.name: getSecurityIsolationStatus
commands.description: Returns information about the COEP/COOP isolation status.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: If no frameId is provided, the status of the target is provided.

commands.name: loadNetworkResource
commands.description: Fetches the resource and returns the content.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description:
Frame id to get the resource for. Mandatory for frame targets, and
should be omitted for worker targets.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL of the resource to get content for.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Options for the request.

commands.name: overrideNetworkState
commands.description: Override the state of navigator.onLine and navigator.connection.

parameters.name: offline
parameters.description: True to emulate internet disconnection.

parameters.name: latency
parameters.description: Minimum latency from request sent to response headers received (ms).

parameters.name: downloadThroughput
parameters.description: Maximal aggregated download throughput (bytes/sec). -1 disables download throttling.

parameters.name: uploadThroughput
parameters.description: Maximal aggregated upload throughput (bytes/sec).  -1 disables upload throttling.

parameters.name: connectionType
parameters.description: Connection type if known.

commands.name: replayXHR
commands.description:
This method sends a new XMLHttpRequest which is identical to the original one. The following
parameters should be identical: method, url, async, request body, extra headers, withCredentials
attribute, user, password.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Identifier of XHR to replay.

commands.name: searchInResponseBody
commands.description: Searches for given string in response content.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Identifier of the network response to search.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: String to search for.

parameters.name: caseSensitive
parameters.description: If true, search is case sensitive.

parameters.name: isRegex
parameters.description: If true, treats string parameter as regex.

returns.name: result
returns.description: List of search matches.

commands.name: setAcceptedEncodings
commands.description: Sets a list of content encodings that will be accepted. Empty list means no encoding is accepted.

parameters.name: encodings
parameters.description: List of accepted content encodings.

commands.name: setAttachDebugStack
commands.description: Specifies whether to attach a page script stack id in requests

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether to attach a page script stack for debugging purpose.

commands.name: setBlockedURLs
commands.description: Blocks URLs from loading.

parameters.name: urlPatterns
parameters.description:
Patterns to match in the order in which they are given. These patterns
also take precedence over any wildcard patterns defined in `urls`.

parameters.name: urls
parameters.description: URL patterns to block. Wildcards ('*') are allowed.

commands.name: setBypassServiceWorker
commands.description: Toggles ignoring of service worker for each request.

parameters.name: bypass
parameters.description: Bypass service worker and load from network.

commands.name: setCacheDisabled
commands.description: Toggles ignoring cache for each request. If `true`, cache will not be used.

parameters.name: cacheDisabled
parameters.description: Cache disabled state.

commands.name: setCookie
commands.description: Sets a cookie with the given cookie data; may overwrite equivalent cookies if they exist.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Cookie name.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Cookie value.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description:
The request-URI to associate with the setting of the cookie. This value can affect the
default domain, path, source port, and source scheme values of the created cookie.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Cookie domain.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Cookie path.

parameters.name: secure
parameters.description: True if cookie is secure.

parameters.name: httpOnly
parameters.description: True if cookie is http-only.

parameters.name: sameSite
parameters.description: Cookie SameSite type.

parameters.name: expires
parameters.description: Cookie expiration date, session cookie if not set

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description: Cookie Priority type.

parameters.name: sourceScheme
parameters.description: Cookie source scheme type.

parameters.name: sourcePort
parameters.description:
Cookie source port. Valid values are {-1, [1, 65535]}, -1 indicates an unspecified port.
An unspecified port value allows protocol clients to emulate legacy cookie scope for the port.
This is a temporary ability and it will be removed in the future.

parameters.name: partitionKey
parameters.description: Cookie partition key. If not set, the cookie will be set as not partitioned.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Always set to true. If an error occurs, the response indicates protocol error.

commands.name: setCookieControls
commands.description:
Sets Controls for third-party cookie access
Page reload is required before the new cookie behavior will be observed

parameters.name: enableThirdPartyCookieRestriction
parameters.description: Whether 3pc restriction is enabled.

parameters.name: disableThirdPartyCookieMetadata
parameters.description: Whether 3pc grace period exception should be enabled; false by default.

parameters.name: disableThirdPartyCookieHeuristics
parameters.description: Whether 3pc heuristics exceptions should be enabled; false by default.

commands.name: setCookies
commands.description: Sets given cookies.

parameters.name: cookies
parameters.description: Cookies to be set.

commands.name: setExtraHTTPHeaders
commands.description: Specifies whether to always send extra HTTP headers with the requests from this page.

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description: Map with extra HTTP headers.

commands.name: setRequestInterception
commands.description:
Sets the requests to intercept that match the provided patterns and optionally resource types.
Deprecated, please use Fetch.enable instead.

parameters.name: patterns
parameters.description:
Requests matching any of these patterns will be forwarded and wait for the corresponding
continueInterceptedRequest call.

commands.name: setUserAgentOverride
commands.description: Allows overriding user agent with the given string.

parameters.name: userAgent
parameters.description: User agent to use.

parameters.name: acceptLanguage
parameters.description: Browser language to emulate.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: The platform navigator.platform should return.

parameters.name: userAgentMetadata
parameters.description: To be sent in Sec-CH-UA-* headers and returned in navigator.userAgentData

commands.name: streamResourceContent
commands.description:
Enables streaming of the response for the given requestId.
If enabled, the dataReceived event contains the data that was received during streaming.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Identifier of the request to stream.

returns.name: bufferedData
returns.description: Data that has been buffered until streaming is enabled. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

commands.name: takeResponseBodyForInterceptionAsStream
commands.description:
Returns a handle to the stream representing the response body. Note that after this command,
the intercepted request can't be continued as is -- you either need to cancel it or to provide
the response body. The stream only supports sequential read, IO.read will fail if the position
is specified.

parameters.name: encodedDataLength

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Data that was received. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

parameters.name: sessionId

The session ID this event is associated with. May not be populated for
failed events.

parameters.name: creationEventDetails
parameters.description: The below are the different session event type details. Exactly one is populated.

events.name: deviceBoundSessionsAdded
events.description: Triggered when the initial set of device bound sessions is added.

parameters.name: sessions
parameters.description: The device bound sessions.

events.name: directTCPSocketChunkReceived

events.description: Fired when direct_socket.UDPSocket connection is opened.

parameters.name: localPort

events.name: eventSourceMessageReceived

parameters.name: requestId

events.name: loadingFailed

parameters.name: requestId

parameters.description: True if loading was canceled.

parameters.description: The reason why loading was blocked, if any.

events.name: loadingFinished

parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.description: Timestamp.

events.name: policyUpdated
events.description: Fired once security policy has been updated.

parameters.description: Origin of the document(s) which configured the endpoints.

events.name: reportingApiReportAdded
events.description:
Is sent whenever a new report is added.
And after 'enableReportingApi' for all existing reports.

events.name: requestIntercepted
events.description:
Details of an intercepted HTTP request, which must be either allowed, blocked, modified or
mocked.
Deprecated, use Fetch.requestPaused instead.

parameters.name: interceptionId
parameters.description:
Each request the page makes will have a unique id, however if any redirects are encountered
while processing that fetch, they will be reported with the same id as the original fetch.
Likewise if HTTP authentication is needed then the same fetch id will be used.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: The id of the frame that initiated the request.

parameters.name: resourceType
parameters.description: How the requested resource will be used.

parameters.name: isNavigationRequest
parameters.description: Whether this is a navigation request, which can abort the navigation completely.

parameters.name: isDownload
parameters.description:
Set if the request is a navigation that will result in a download.
Only present after response is received from the server (i.e. HeadersReceived stage).

parameters.name: redirectUrl
parameters.description: Redirect location, only sent if a redirect was intercepted.

parameters.name: authChallenge
parameters.description:
Details of the Authorization Challenge encountered. If this is set then
continueInterceptedRequest must contain an authChallengeResponse.

parameters.name: responseErrorReason
parameters.description:
Response error if intercepted at response stage or if redirect occurred while intercepting
request.

parameters.name: responseStatusCode
parameters.description:
Response code if intercepted at response stage or if redirect occurred while intercepting
request or auth retry occurred.

parameters.name: responseHeaders
parameters.description:
Response headers if intercepted at the response stage or if redirect occurred while
intercepting request or auth retry occurred.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description:
If the intercepted request had a corresponding requestWillBeSent event fired for it, then
this requestId will be the same as the requestId present in the requestWillBeSent event.

events.name: requestServedFromCache
events.description: Fired if request ended up loading from cache.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

events.name: requestWillBeSent
events.description: Fired when page is about to send HTTP request.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: loaderId
parameters.description: Loader identifier. Empty string if the request is fetched from worker.

parameters.name: documentURL
parameters.description: URL of the document this request is loaded for.

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: Request data.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: wallTime
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: initiator
parameters.description: Request initiator.

parameters.name: redirectHasExtraInfo
parameters.description:
In the case that redirectResponse is populated, this flag indicates whether
requestWillBeSentExtraInfo and responseReceivedExtraInfo events will be or were emitted
for the request which was just redirected.

parameters.name: redirectResponse
parameters.description: Redirect response data.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of this resource.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Frame identifier.

parameters.name: hasUserGesture
parameters.description: Whether the request is initiated by a user gesture. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: renderBlockingBehavior
parameters.description: The render-blocking behavior of the request.

events.name: requestWillBeSentExtraInfo
events.description:
Fired when additional information about a requestWillBeSent event is available from the
network stack. Not every requestWillBeSent event will have an additional
requestWillBeSentExtraInfo fired for it, and there is no guarantee whether requestWillBeSent
or requestWillBeSentExtraInfo will be fired first for the same request.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier. Used to match this information to an existing requestWillBeSent event.

parameters.name: associatedCookies
parameters.description:
A list of cookies potentially associated to the requested URL. This includes both cookies sent with
the request and the ones not sent; the latter are distinguished by having blockedReasons field set.

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description: Raw request headers as they will be sent over the wire.

parameters.name: connectTiming
parameters.description: Connection timing information for the request.

parameters.name: deviceBoundSessionUsages
parameters.description: How the request site's device bound sessions were used during this request.

parameters.name: clientSecurityState
parameters.description: The client security state set for the request.

parameters.name: siteHasCookieInOtherPartition
parameters.description: Whether the site has partitioned cookies stored in a partition different than the current one.

parameters.name: appliedNetworkConditionsId
parameters.description:
The network conditions id if this request was affected by network conditions configured via
emulateNetworkConditionsByRule.

events.name: resourceChangedPriority
events.description: Fired when resource loading priority is changed

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: newPriority
parameters.description: New priority

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

events.name: responseReceived
events.description: Fired when HTTP response is available.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: loaderId
parameters.description: Loader identifier. Empty string if the request is fetched from worker.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Resource type.

parameters.name: response
parameters.description: Response data.

parameters.name: hasExtraInfo
parameters.description:
Indicates whether requestWillBeSentExtraInfo and responseReceivedExtraInfo events will be
or were emitted for this request.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Frame identifier.

events.name: responseReceivedEarlyHints
events.description:
Fired when 103 Early Hints headers is received in addition to the common response.
Not every responseReceived event will have an responseReceivedEarlyHints fired.
Only one responseReceivedEarlyHints may be fired for eached responseReceived event.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier. Used to match this information to another responseReceived event.

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description:
Raw response headers as they were received over the wire.
Duplicate headers in the response are represented as a single key with their values
concatentated using `\n` as the separator.
See also `headersText` that contains verbatim text for HTTP/1.*.

events.name: responseReceivedExtraInfo
events.description:
Fired when additional information about a responseReceived event is available from the network
stack. Not every responseReceived event will have an additional responseReceivedExtraInfo for
it, and responseReceivedExtraInfo may be fired before or after responseReceived.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier. Used to match this information to another responseReceived event.

parameters.name: blockedCookies
parameters.description:
A list of cookies which were not stored from the response along with the corresponding
reasons for blocking. The cookies here may not be valid due to syntax errors, which
are represented by the invalid cookie line string instead of a proper cookie.

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description:
Raw response headers as they were received over the wire.
Duplicate headers in the response are represented as a single key with their values
concatentated using `\n` as the separator.
See also `headersText` that contains verbatim text for HTTP/1.*.

parameters.name: resourceIPAddressSpace
parameters.description:
The IP address space of the resource. The address space can only be determined once the transport
established the connection, so we can't send it in `requestWillBeSentExtraInfo`.

parameters.name: statusCode
parameters.description:
The status code of the response. This is useful in cases the request failed and no responseReceived
event is triggered, which is the case for, e.g., CORS errors. This is also the correct status code
for cached requests, where the status in responseReceived is a 200 and this will be 304.

parameters.name: headersText
parameters.description:
Raw response header text as it was received over the wire. The raw text may not always be
available, such as in the case of HTTP/2 or QUIC.

parameters.name: cookiePartitionKey
parameters.description:
The cookie partition key that will be used to store partitioned cookies set in this response.
Only sent when partitioned cookies are enabled.

parameters.name: cookiePartitionKeyOpaque
parameters.description: True if partitioned cookies are enabled, but the partition key is not serializable to string.

parameters.name: exemptedCookies
parameters.description:
A list of cookies which should have been blocked by 3PCD but are exempted and stored from
the response with the corresponding reason.

events.name: signedExchangeReceived
events.description: Fired when a signed exchange was received over the network

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: Request identifier.

parameters.name: info
parameters.description: Information about the signed exchange response.

events.name: trustTokenOperationDone
events.description:
Fired exactly once for each Trust Token operation. Depending on
the type of the operation and whether the operation succeeded or
failed, the event is fired before the corresponding request was sent
or after the response was received.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
Detailed success or error status of the operation.
'AlreadyExists' also signifies a successful operation, as the result
of the operation already exists und thus, the operation was abort
preemptively (e.g. a cache hit).

parameters.name: topLevelOrigin
parameters.description: Top level origin. The context in which the operation was attempted.

parameters.name: issuerOrigin
parameters.description: Origin of the issuer in case of a "Issuance" or "Redemption" operation.

parameters.name: issuedTokenCount
parameters.description: The number of obtained Trust Tokens on a successful "Issuance" operation.

types.name: Network.AdAncestry
types.description:
Encapsulates the script ancestry and the root script filter list rule that
caused the resource or element to be labeled as an ad.

properties.name: ancestryChain
properties.description:
A chain of `AdScriptIdentifier`s representing the ancestry of an ad
script that led to the creation of a resource or element. The chain is
ordered from the script itself (lowest level) up to its root ancestor
that was flagged by a filter list.

properties.name: rootScriptFilterlistRule
properties.description:
The filter list rule that caused the root (last) script in
`ancestryChain` to be tagged as an ad.

types.name: Network.AdProvenance
types.description:
Represents the provenance of an ad resource or element. Only one of
`filterlistRule` or `adScriptAncestry` can be set. If `filterlistRule`
is provided, the resource URL directly matches a filter list rule. If
`adScriptAncestry` is provided, an ad script initiated the resource fetch or
appended the element to the DOM. If neither is provided, the entity is
known to be an ad, but provenance tracking information is unavailable.

properties.name: filterlistRule
properties.description: The filterlist rule that matched, if any.

properties.name: adScriptAncestry
properties.description: The script ancestry that created the ad, if any.

types.name: Network.AdScriptIdentifier
types.description:
Identifies the script on the stack that caused a resource or element to be
labeled as an ad. For resources, this indicates the context that triggered
the fetch. For elements, this indicates the context that caused the element
to be appended to the DOM.

properties.name: scriptId
properties.description: The script's V8 identifier.

properties.name: debuggerId
properties.description: V8's debugging ID for the v8::Context.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The script's url (or generated name based on id if inline script).

types.name: Network.AlternateProtocolUsage
types.description: The reason why Chrome uses a specific transport protocol for HTTP semantics.

types.name: Network.AssociatedCookie
types.description:
A cookie associated with the request which may or may not be sent with it.
Includes the cookies itself and reasons for blocking or exemption.

properties.name: cookie
properties.description: The cookie object representing the cookie which was not sent.

properties.name: blockedReasons
properties.description: The reason(s) the cookie was blocked. If empty means the cookie is included.

properties.name: exemptionReason
properties.description:
The reason the cookie should have been blocked by 3PCD but is exempted. A cookie could
only have at most one exemption reason.

types.name: Network.AuthChallenge
types.description: Authorization challenge for HTTP status code 401 or 407.

properties.name: source
properties.description: Source of the authentication challenge.

properties.name: origin
properties.description: Origin of the challenger.

properties.name: scheme
properties.description: The authentication scheme used, such as basic or digest

properties.name: realm
properties.description: The realm of the challenge. May be empty.

types.name: Network.AuthChallengeResponse
types.description: Response to an AuthChallenge.

properties.name: response
properties.description:
The decision on what to do in response to the authorization challenge.  Default means
deferring to the default behavior of the net stack, which will likely either the Cancel
authentication or display a popup dialog box.

properties.name: username
properties.description:
The username to provide, possibly empty. Should only be set if response is
ProvideCredentials.

properties.name: password
properties.description:
The password to provide, possibly empty. Should only be set if response is
ProvideCredentials.

types.name: Network.BlockedReason
types.description: The reason why request was blocked.

types.name: Network.BlockedSetCookieWithReason
types.description: A cookie which was not stored from a response with the corresponding reason.

properties.name: blockedReasons
properties.description: The reason(s) this cookie was blocked.

properties.name: cookieLine
properties.description:
The string representing this individual cookie as it would appear in the header.
This is not the entire "cookie" or "set-cookie" header which could have multiple cookies.

properties.name: cookie
properties.description:
The cookie object which represents the cookie which was not stored. It is optional because
sometimes complete cookie information is not available, such as in the case of parsing
errors.

properties.name: urlPattern
properties.description:
URL pattern to match. Patterns use the URLPattern constructor string syntax
(https://urlpattern.spec.whatwg.org/) and must be absolute. Example: `*://*:*/*.css`.

properties.name: block
properties.description:
Whether or not to block the pattern. If false, a matching request will not be blocked even if it matches a later
`BlockPattern`.

Timing's requestTime is a baseline in seconds, while the other numbers are ticks in

the same request (but not for redirected requests).

properties.name: path
properties.description: Cookie path.

properties.name: expires
properties.description:
Cookie expiration date as the number of seconds since the UNIX epoch.
The value is set to -1 if the expiry date is not set.
The value can be null for values that cannot be represented in
JSON (±Inf).

properties.name: size
properties.description: Cookie size.

properties.name: httpOnly
properties.description: True if cookie is http-only.

properties.name: secure
properties.description: True if cookie is secure.

properties.name: session
properties.description: True in case of session cookie.

properties.name: sameSite

properties.name: priority
properties.description: Cookie Priority

properties.name: sourceScheme
properties.description: Cookie source scheme type.

properties.name: sourcePort
properties.description:
Cookie source port. Valid values are {-1, [1, 65535]}, -1 indicates an unspecified port.
An unspecified port value allows protocol clients to emulate legacy cookie scope for the port.
This is a temporary ability and it will be removed in the future.

properties.name: partitionKey
properties.description: Cookie partition key.

properties.name: partitionKeyOpaque
properties.description: True if cookie partition key is opaque.

types.name: Network.CookieBlockedReason
types.description: Types of reasons why a cookie may not be sent with a request.

types.name: Network.CookieExemptionReason
types.description: Types of reasons why a cookie should have been blocked by 3PCD but is exempted for the request.

types.name: Network.CookieParam
types.description: Cookie parameter object

properties.name: name
properties.description: Cookie name.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Cookie value.

properties.name: url
properties.description:
The request-URI to associate with the setting of the cookie. This value can affect the
default domain, path, source port, and source scheme values of the created cookie.

properties.name: domain
properties.description: Cookie domain.

properties.name: path
properties.description: Cookie path.

properties.name: secure
properties.description: True if cookie is secure.

properties.name: httpOnly
properties.description: True if cookie is http-only.

properties.name: sameSite
properties.description: Cookie SameSite type.

properties.name: expires
properties.description: Cookie expiration date, session cookie if not set

properties.name: priority
properties.description: Cookie Priority.

properties.name: sourceScheme
properties.description: Cookie source scheme type.

properties.name: sourcePort
properties.description:
Cookie source port. Valid values are {-1, [1, 65535]}, -1 indicates an unspecified port.
An unspecified port value allows protocol clients to emulate legacy cookie scope for the port.
This is a temporary ability and it will be removed in the future.

properties.name: partitionKey
properties.description: Cookie partition key. If not set, the cookie will be set as not partitioned.

types.name: Network.CookiePartitionKey
types.description:
cookiePartitionKey object
The representation of the components of the key that are created by the cookiePartitionKey class contained in net/cookies/cookie_partition_key.h.

properties.description:
The site of the top-level URL the browser was visiting at the start

properties.description: Indicates if the cookie has any ancestors that are cross-site to the topLevelSite.

types.name: Network.CookiePriority
types.description:
Represents the cookie's 'Priority' status:
https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-west-cookie-priority-00

types.name: Network.CookieSameSite
types.description:
Represents the cookie's 'SameSite' status:
https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-west-first-party-cookies

types.name: Network.CookieSourceScheme
types.description:
Represents the source scheme of the origin that originally set the cookie.
A value of "Unset" allows protocol clients to emulate legacy cookie scope for the scheme.
This is a temporary ability and it will be removed in the future.

domains.name: Overlay

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables domain notifications.

commands.name: enable
commands.description: Enables domain notifications.

commands.name: getGridHighlightObjectsForTest
commands.description: For Persistent Grid testing.

parameters.name: nodeIds

returns.description: Grid Highlight data for the node ids provided.

commands.name: getHighlightObjectForTest
commands.description: For testing.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Id of the node to get highlight object for.

parameters.name: includeDistance
parameters.description: Whether to include distance info.

parameters.name: includeStyle
parameters.description: Whether to include style info.

returns.description: Source order highlight data for the node id provided.

commands.name: hideHighlight
commands.description: Hides any highlight.

commands.name: highlightFrame
commands.description:
Highlights owner element of the frame with given id.
Deprecated: Doesn't work reliably and cannot be fixed due to process
separation (the owner node might be in a different process). Determine
the owner node in the client and use highlightNode.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Identifier of the frame to highlight.

parameters.name: contentColor
parameters.description: The content box highlight fill color (default: transparent).

parameters.name: contentOutlineColor
parameters.description: The content box highlight outline color (default: transparent).

commands.name: highlightNode
commands.description:
Highlights DOM node with given id or with the given JavaScript object wrapper. Either nodeId or
objectId must be specified.

parameters.name: highlightConfig
parameters.description: A descriptor for the highlight appearance.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the node to highlight.

parameters.name: backendNodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the backend node to highlight.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: JavaScript object id of the node to be highlighted.

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Selectors to highlight relevant nodes.

commands.name: highlightQuad
commands.description: Highlights given quad. Coordinates are absolute with respect to the main frame viewport.

parameters.name: quad
parameters.description: Quad to highlight

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: The highlight fill color (default: transparent).

parameters.name: outlineColor
parameters.description: The highlight outline color (default: transparent).

commands.name: highlightRect
commands.description:
Highlights given rectangle. Coordinates are absolute with respect to the main frame viewport.
Issue: the method does not handle device pixel ratio (DPR) correctly.
The coordinates currently have to be adjusted by the client
if DPR is not 1 (see crbug.com/437807128).

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: X coordinate

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Y coordinate

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: Rectangle width

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: Rectangle height

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: The highlight fill color (default: transparent).

parameters.name: outlineColor
parameters.description: The highlight outline color (default: transparent).

commands.name: highlightSourceOrder
commands.description:
Highlights the source order of the children of the DOM node with given id or with the given
JavaScript object wrapper. Either nodeId or objectId must be specified.

parameters.name: sourceOrderConfig
parameters.description: A descriptor for the appearance of the overlay drawing.

parameters.name: nodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the node to highlight.

parameters.name: backendNodeId
parameters.description: Identifier of the backend node to highlight.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: JavaScript object id of the node to be highlighted.

commands.name: setInspectMode
commands.description:
Enters the 'inspect' mode. In this mode, elements that user is hovering over are highlighted.
Backend then generates 'inspectNodeRequested' event upon element selection.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Set an inspection mode.

parameters.name: highlightConfig
parameters.description:
A descriptor for the highlight appearance of hovered-over nodes. May be omitted if `enabled
== false`.

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: The message to display, also triggers resume and step over controls.

commands.name: setShowPaintRects

parameters.name: scrollSnapHighlightConfigs

events.name: inspectedElementWindowRestored

user manually inspects an element.

events.name: inspectPanelShowRequested

parameters.description: Id of the node to show in the panel.

events.name: nodeHighlightRequested
events.description: Fired when the node should be highlighted. This happens after call to `setInspectMode`.

events.name: screenshotRequested
events.description: Fired when user asks to capture screenshot of some area on the page.

parameters.name: viewport
parameters.description: Viewport to capture, in device independent pixels (dip).
