domains.name: Page
domains.description: Actions and events related to the inspected page belong to the page domain.

commands.name: addCompilationCache
commands.description:
Seeds compilation cache for given url. Compilation cache does not survive
cross-process navigation.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Base64-encoded data (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

commands.name: addScriptToEvaluateOnLoad
commands.description: Deprecated, please use addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument instead.

returns.name: identifier
returns.description: Identifier of the added script.

commands.name: addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument
commands.description: Evaluates given script in every frame upon creation (before loading frame's scripts).

parameters.name: worldName
parameters.description:
If specified, creates an isolated world with the given name and evaluates given script in it.
This world name will be used as the ExecutionContextDescription::name when the corresponding
event is emitted.

parameters.name: includeCommandLineAPI
parameters.description:
Specifies whether command line API should be available to the script, defaults
to false.

parameters.name: runImmediately
parameters.description:
If true, runs the script immediately on existing execution contexts or worlds.
Default: false.

returns.name: identifier
returns.description: Identifier of the added script.

commands.name: bringToFront
commands.description: Brings page to front (activates tab).

commands.name: captureScreenshot
commands.description: Capture page screenshot.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Image compression format (defaults to png).

parameters.name: quality
parameters.description: Compression quality from range [0..100] (jpeg only).

parameters.name: clip
parameters.description: Capture the screenshot of a given region only.

parameters.name: fromSurface
parameters.description: Capture the screenshot from the surface, rather than the view. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: captureBeyondViewport
parameters.description: Capture the screenshot beyond the viewport. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: optimizeForSpeed
parameters.description: Optimize image encoding for speed, not for resulting size (defaults to false)

returns.name: data
returns.description: Base64-encoded image data. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

commands.name: captureSnapshot
commands.description:
Returns a snapshot of the page as a string. For MHTML format, the serialization includes
iframes, shadow DOM, external resources, and element-inline styles.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Format (defaults to mhtml).

returns.name: data
returns.description: Serialized page data.

commands.name: clearCompilationCache
commands.description: Clears seeded compilation cache.

commands.name: clearDeviceMetricsOverride
commands.description: Clears the overridden device metrics.

commands.name: clearDeviceOrientationOverride
commands.description: Clears the overridden Device Orientation.

commands.name: clearGeolocationOverride
commands.description: Clears the overridden Geolocation Position and Error.

commands.name: close
commands.description: Tries to close page, running its beforeunload hooks, if any.

commands.name: crash
commands.description: Crashes renderer on the IO thread, generates minidumps.

commands.name: createIsolatedWorld
commands.description: Creates an isolated world for the given frame.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame in which the isolated world should be created.

parameters.name: worldName
parameters.description: An optional name which is reported in the Execution Context.

parameters.name: grantUniveralAccess
parameters.description:
Whether or not universal access should be granted to the isolated world. This is a powerful
option, use with caution.

returns.name: executionContextId
returns.description: Execution context of the isolated world.

commands.name: deleteCookie
commands.description: Deletes browser cookie with given name, domain and path.

parameters.name: cookieName
parameters.description: Name of the cookie to remove.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL to match cooke domain and path.

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables page domain notifications.

commands.name: enable
commands.description: Enables page domain notifications.

parameters.name: enableFileChooserOpenedEvent
parameters.description:
If true, the `Page.fileChooserOpened` event will be emitted regardless of the state set by
`Page.setInterceptFileChooserDialog` command (default: false).

commands.name: generateTestReport
commands.description: Generates a report for testing.

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: Message to be displayed in the report.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Specifies the endpoint group to deliver the report to.

returns.name: adScriptAncestry
returns.description:
The ancestry chain of ad script identifiers leading to this frame's
creation, along with the root script's filterlist rule. The ancestry
chain is ordered from the most immediate script (in the frame creation
stack) to more distant ancestors (that created the immediately preceding
script). Only sent if frame is labelled as an ad and ids are available.

commands.name: getAnnotatedPageContent
commands.description:
Get the annotated page content for the main frame.
This is an experimental command that is subject to change.

parameters.name: includeActionableInformation
parameters.description: Whether to include actionable information. Defaults to true.

returns.name: content
returns.description:
The annotated page content as a base64 encoded protobuf.
The format is defined by the `AnnotatedPageContent` message in
components/optimization_guide/proto/features/common_quality_data.proto (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

commands.name: getAppId
commands.description:
Returns the unique (PWA) app id.
Only returns values if the feature flag 'WebAppEnableManifestId' is enabled

returns.name: appId
returns.description: App id, either from manifest's id attribute or computed from start_url

returns.name: recommendedId

commands.name: getAppManifest

Gets the processed manifest for this current document.

  If manifestId is provided, and it does not match the manifest of the
    current document, this API errors out.
  If there is not a loaded page, this API errors out immediately.

returns.name: url
returns.description: Manifest location.

returns.name: data
returns.description: Manifest content.

returns.name: parsed
returns.description: Parsed manifest properties. Deprecated, use manifest instead.

commands.name: printToPDF
commands.description: Print page as PDF.

parameters.name: landscape
parameters.description: Paper orientation. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: displayHeaderFooter
parameters.description: Display header and footer. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: printBackground
parameters.description: Print background graphics. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Scale of the webpage rendering. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: paperWidth
parameters.description: Paper width in inches. Defaults to 8.5 inches.

parameters.name: paperHeight
parameters.description: Paper height in inches. Defaults to 11 inches.

parameters.name: marginTop
parameters.description: Top margin in inches. Defaults to 1cm (~0.4 inches).

parameters.name: marginBottom
parameters.description: Bottom margin in inches. Defaults to 1cm (~0.4 inches).

parameters.name: marginLeft
parameters.description: Left margin in inches. Defaults to 1cm (~0.4 inches).

parameters.name: marginRight
parameters.description: Right margin in inches. Defaults to 1cm (~0.4 inches).

parameters.name: pageRanges
parameters.description:
Paper ranges to print, one based, e.g., '1-5, 8, 11-13'. Pages are
printed in the document order, not in the order specified, and no
more than once.
Defaults to empty string, which implies the entire document is printed.
The page numbers are quietly capped to actual page count of the
document, and ranges beyond the end of the document are ignored.
If this results in no pages to print, an error is reported.
It is an error to specify a range with start greater than end.

parameters.name: headerTemplate
parameters.description: HTML template for the print header. Should be valid HTML markup with following classes used to inject printing values into them: - `date`: formatted print date - `title`: document title - `url`: document location - `pageNumber`: current page number - `totalPages`: total pages in the document For example, `` would generate span containing the title.

parameters.name: footerTemplate
parameters.description: HTML template for the print footer. Should use the same format as the `headerTemplate`.

parameters.name: preferCSSPageSize
parameters.description:
Whether or not to prefer page size as defined by css. Defaults to false,
in which case the content will be scaled to fit the paper size.

parameters.name: transferMode
parameters.description: return as stream

parameters.name: generateTaggedPDF
parameters.description: Whether or not to generate tagged (accessible) PDF. Defaults to embedder choice.

parameters.name: generateDocumentOutline
parameters.description: Whether or not to embed the document outline into the PDF.

returns.name: data
returns.description: Base64-encoded pdf data. Empty if |returnAsStream| is specified. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

returns.name: stream
returns.description: A handle of the stream that holds resulting PDF data.

commands.name: produceCompilationCache
commands.description:
Requests backend to produce compilation cache for the specified scripts.
`scripts` are appended to the list of scripts for which the cache
would be produced. The list may be reset during page navigation.
When script with a matching URL is encountered, the cache is optionally
produced upon backend discretion, based on internal heuristics.
See also: `Page.compilationCacheProduced`.

commands.name: reload
commands.description: Reloads given page optionally ignoring the cache.

parameters.name: ignoreCache
parameters.description: If true, browser cache is ignored (as if the user pressed Shift+refresh).

parameters.name: scriptToEvaluateOnLoad
parameters.description:
If set, the script will be injected into all frames of the inspected page after reload.
Argument will be ignored if reloading dataURL origin.

parameters.name: loaderId
parameters.description:
If set, an error will be thrown if the target page's main frame's
loader id does not match the provided id. This prevents accidentally
reloading an unintended target in case there's a racing navigation.

commands.name: removeScriptToEvaluateOnLoad
commands.description: Deprecated, please use removeScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument instead.

commands.name: removeScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument
commands.description: Removes given script from the list.

commands.name: resetNavigationHistory
commands.description: Resets navigation history for the current page.

returns.name: result

events.description:
Fired when a renderer-initiated navigation is requested.
Navigation may still be cancelled after the event is issued.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame that is being navigated.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: The reason for the navigation.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The destination URL for the requested navigation.

parameters.name: disposition
parameters.description: The disposition for the navigation.

events.description: Fired when frame schedules a potential navigation.

parameters.description: Id of the frame that has scheduled a navigation.

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description:
Delay (in seconds) until the navigation is scheduled to begin. The navigation is not
guaranteed to start.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: The reason for the navigation.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The destination URL for the scheduled navigation.

events.name: frameStartedLoading
events.description: Fired when frame has started loading.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Id of the frame that has started loading.

events.name: frameStartedNavigating
events.description:
Fired when a navigation starts. This event is fired for both
renderer-initiated and browser-initiated navigations. For renderer-initiated
navigations, the event is fired after `frameRequestedNavigation`.
Navigation may still be cancelled after the event is issued. Multiple events
can be fired for a single navigation, for example, when a same-document
navigation becomes a cross-document navigation (such as in the case of a
frameset).

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: ID of the frame that is being navigated.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL the navigation started with. The final URL can be different.

parameters.name: loaderId
parameters.description:
Loader identifier. Even though it is present in case of same-document
navigation, the previously committed loaderId would not change unless
the navigation changes from a same-document to a cross-document
navigation.

events.description: Fired when frame has stopped loading.

parameters.description: Id of the frame that has stopped loading.

Fired before frame subtree is detached. Emitted before any frame of the

types.name: Preload.PreloadingAttemptKey
types.description:
A key that identifies a preloading attempt.

The url used is the url specified by the trigger (i.e. the initial URL), and
not the final url that is navigated to. For example, prerendering allows
same-origin main frame navigations during the attempt, but the attempt is
still keyed with the initial URL.

types.name: Preload.PreloadingAttemptSource
types.description:
Lists sources for a preloading attempt, specifically the ids of rule sets
that had a speculation rule that triggered the attempt, and the
BackendNodeIds of <a href> or <area href> elements that triggered the
attempt (in the case of attempts triggered by a document rule). It is
possible for multiple rule sets and links to trigger a single attempt.

types.name: Preload.PreloadingStatus
types.description:
Preloading status values, see also PreloadingTriggeringOutcome. This
status is shared by prefetchStatusUpdated and prerenderStatusUpdated.

types.name: Preload.PreloadPipelineId
types.description:
Chrome manages different types of preloads together using a
concept of preloading pipeline. For example, if a site uses a
SpeculationRules for prerender, Chrome first starts a prefetch and
then upgrades it to prerender.

CDP events for them are emitted separately but they share
`PreloadPipelineId`.

properties.description:
Source text of JSON representing the rule set. If it comes from
`<script>` tag, it is the textContent of the node. Note that it is
a JSON for valid case.

See also:
- https://wicg.github.io/nav-speculation/speculation-rules.html
- https://github.com/WICG/nav-speculation/blob/main/triggers.md

properties.name: backendNodeId
properties.description:
A speculation rule set is either added through an inline
`<script>` tag or through an external resource via the
'Speculation-Rules' HTTP header. For the first case, we include
the BackendNodeId of the relevant `<script>` tag. For the second
case, we include the external URL where the rule set was loaded
from, and also RequestId if Network domain is enabled.

See also:
- https://wicg.github.io/nav-speculation/speculation-rules.html#speculation-rules-script
- https://wicg.github.io/nav-speculation/speculation-rules.html#speculation-rules-header

Enable precise code coverage. Coverage data for JavaScript executed before enabling precise code

parameters.name: occasion

domains.name: PWA
domains.description: This domain allows interacting with the browser to control PWAs.

commands.name: changeAppUserSettings
commands.description:
Changes user settings of the web app identified by its manifestId. If the
app was not installed, this command returns an error. Unset parameters will
be ignored; unrecognized values will cause an error.

Unlike the ones defined in the manifest files of the web apps, these
settings are provided by the browser and controlled by the users, they
impact the way the browser handling the web apps.

See the comment of each parameter.

parameters.name: linkCapturing
parameters.description:
If user allows the links clicked on by the user in the app's scope, or
extended scope if the manifest has scope extensions and the flags
`DesktopPWAsLinkCapturingWithScopeExtensions` and
`WebAppEnableScopeExtensions` are enabled.

Note, the API does not support resetting the linkCapturing to the
initial value, uninstalling and installing the web app again will reset
it.

TODO(crbug.com/339453269): Setting this value on ChromeOS is not
supported yet.

commands.name: getOsAppState
commands.description: Returns the following OS state for the given manifest id.

parameters.name: manifestId
parameters.description:
The id from the webapp's manifest file, commonly it's the url of the
site installing the webapp. See
https://web.dev/learn/pwa/web-app-manifest.

commands.name: install
commands.description:
Installs the given manifest identity, optionally using the given installUrlOrBundleUrl

IWA-specific install description:
manifestId corresponds to isolated-app:// + web_package::SignedWebBundleId

File installation mode:
The installUrlOrBundleUrl can be either file:// or http(s):// pointing
to a signed web bundle (.swbn). In this case SignedWebBundleId must correspond to
The .swbn file's signing key.

Dev proxy installation mode:
installUrlOrBundleUrl must be http(s):// that serves dev mode IWA.
web_package::SignedWebBundleId must be of type dev proxy.

The advantage of dev proxy mode is that all changes to IWA
automatically will be reflected in the running app without
reinstallation.

To generate bundle id for proxy mode:
1. Generate 32 random bytes.
2. Add a specific suffix at the end following the documentation
   https://github.com/WICG/isolated-web-apps/blob/main/Scheme.md#suffix
3. Encode the entire sequence using Base32 without padding.

If Chrome is not in IWA dev
mode, the installation will fail, regardless of the state of the allowlist.

parameters.name: installUrlOrBundleUrl
parameters.description:
The location of the app or bundle overriding the one derived from the
manifestId.

commands.name: launch
commands.description:
Launches the installed web app, or an url in the same web app instead of the
default start url if it is provided. Returns a page Target.TargetID which
can be used to attach to via Target.attachToTarget or similar APIs.

returns.name: targetId
returns.description: ID of the tab target created as a result.

commands.name: launchFilesInApp
commands.description:
Opens one or more local files from an installed web app identified by its
manifestId. The web app needs to have file handlers registered to process
the files. The API returns one or more page Target.TargetIDs which can be
used to attach to via Target.attachToTarget or similar APIs.
If some files in the parameters cannot be handled by the web app, they will
be ignored. If none of the files can be handled, this API returns an error.
If no files are provided as the parameter, this API also returns an error.

According to the definition of the file handlers in the manifest file, one
Target.TargetID may represent a page handling one or more files. The order
of the returned Target.TargetIDs is not guaranteed.

TODO(crbug.com/339454034): Check the existences of the input files.

returns.name: targetIds
returns.description: IDs of the tab targets created as the result.

commands.name: openCurrentPageInApp
commands.description:
Opens the current page in its web app identified by the manifest id, needs
to be called on a page target. This function returns immediately without
waiting for the app to finish loading.

commands.name: uninstall
commands.description: Uninstalls the given manifest_id and closes any opened app windows.

types.name: PWA.DisplayMode
types.description: If user prefers opening the app in browser or an app window.

types.name: PWA.FileHandlerAccept
types.description:
The following types are the replica of
https://crsrc.org/c/chrome/browser/web_applications/proto/web_app_os_integration_state.proto;drc=9910d3be894c8f142c977ba1023f30a656bc13fc;l=67

properties.name: mediaType
properties.description:
New name of the mimetype according to
https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/media-types.xhtml

domains.name: Runtime
domains.description:
Runtime domain exposes JavaScript runtime by means of remote evaluation and mirror objects.
Evaluation results are returned as mirror object that expose object type, string representation
and unique identifier that can be used for further object reference. Original objects are
maintained in memory unless they are either explicitly released or are released along with the
other objects in their object group.

commands.name: addBinding
commands.description:
If executionContextId is empty, adds binding with the given name on the
global objects of all inspected contexts, including those created later,
bindings survive reloads.
Binding function takes exactly one argument, this argument should be string,
in case of any other input, function throws an exception.
Each binding function call produces Runtime.bindingCalled notification.

parameters.name: executionContextId
parameters.description:
If specified, the binding would only be exposed to the specified
execution context. If omitted and `executionContextName` is not set,
the binding is exposed to all execution contexts of the target.
This parameter is mutually exclusive with `executionContextName`.
Deprecated in favor of `executionContextName` due to an unclear use case
and bugs in implementation (crbug.com/1169639). `executionContextId` will be
removed in the future.

parameters.name: executionContextName
parameters.description:
If specified, the binding is exposed to the executionContext with
matching name, even for contexts created after the binding is added.
See also `ExecutionContext.name` and `worldName` parameter to
`Page.addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument`.
This parameter is mutually exclusive with `executionContextId`.

commands.name: awaitPromise
commands.description: Add handler to promise with given promise object id.

parameters.name: promiseObjectId
parameters.description: Identifier of the promise.

parameters.name: returnByValue
parameters.description: Whether the result is expected to be a JSON object that should be sent by value.

parameters.name: generatePreview
parameters.description: Whether preview should be generated for the result.

returns.name: result
returns.description: Promise result. Will contain rejected value if promise was rejected.

returns.name: exceptionDetails
returns.description: Exception details if stack strace is available.

commands.name: callFunctionOn
commands.description:
Calls function with given declaration on the given object. Object group of the result is
inherited from the target object.

parameters.name: functionDeclaration
parameters.description: Declaration of the function to call.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description:
Identifier of the object to call function on. Either objectId or executionContextId should
be specified.

parameters.name: arguments
parameters.description:
Call arguments. All call arguments must belong to the same JavaScript world as the target
object.

parameters.name: silent
parameters.description:
In silent mode exceptions thrown during evaluation are not reported and do not pause
execution. Overrides `setPauseOnException` state.

parameters.name: returnByValue
parameters.description:
Whether the result is expected to be a JSON object which should be sent by value.
Can be overriden by `serializationOptions`.

parameters.name: generatePreview
parameters.description: Whether preview should be generated for the result.

parameters.name: userGesture
parameters.description: Whether execution should be treated as initiated by user in the UI.

parameters.name: awaitPromise
parameters.description:
Whether execution should `await` for resulting value and return once awaited promise is
resolved.

parameters.name: executionContextId
parameters.description:
Specifies execution context which global object will be used to call function on. Either
executionContextId or objectId should be specified.

parameters.name: objectGroup
parameters.description:
Symbolic group name that can be used to release multiple objects. If objectGroup is not
specified and objectId is, objectGroup will be inherited from object.

parameters.name: throwOnSideEffect
parameters.description: Whether to throw an exception if side effect cannot be ruled out during evaluation.

parameters.name: uniqueContextId
parameters.description:
An alternative way to specify the execution context to call function on.
Compared to contextId that may be reused across processes, this is guaranteed to be
system-unique, so it can be used to prevent accidental function call
in context different than intended (e.g. as a result of navigation across process
boundaries).
This is mutually exclusive with `executionContextId`.

parameters.name: serializationOptions
parameters.description:
Specifies the result serialization. If provided, overrides
`generatePreview` and `returnByValue`.

returns.name: result
returns.description: Call result.

returns.name: exceptionDetails
returns.description: Exception details.

commands.name: compileScript
commands.description: Compiles expression.

parameters.name: expression
parameters.description: Expression to compile.

parameters.name: sourceURL
parameters.description: Source url to be set for the script.

parameters.name: persistScript
parameters.description: Specifies whether the compiled script should be persisted.

parameters.name: executionContextId
parameters.description:
Specifies in which execution context to perform script run. If the parameter is omitted the
evaluation will be performed in the context of the inspected page.

returns.name: scriptId
returns.description: Id of the script.

returns.name: exceptionDetails
returns.description: Exception details.

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables reporting of execution contexts creation.

commands.name: discardConsoleEntries
commands.description: Discards collected exceptions and console API calls.

commands.name: enable
commands.description:
Enables reporting of execution contexts creation by means of `executionContextCreated` event.
When the reporting gets enabled the event will be sent immediately for each existing execution
context.

commands.name: evaluate
commands.description: Evaluates expression on global object.

parameters.name: expression
parameters.description: Expression to evaluate.

parameters.name: objectGroup
parameters.description: Symbolic group name that can be used to release multiple objects.

parameters.name: includeCommandLineAPI
parameters.description: Determines whether Command Line API should be available during the evaluation.

parameters.name: silent
parameters.description:
In silent mode exceptions thrown during evaluation are not reported and do not pause
execution. Overrides `setPauseOnException` state.

parameters.name: contextId
parameters.description:
Specifies in which execution context to perform evaluation. If the parameter is omitted the
evaluation will be performed in the context of the inspected page.
This is mutually exclusive with `uniqueContextId`, which offers an
alternative way to identify the execution context that is more reliable
in a multi-process environment.

parameters.name: returnByValue
parameters.description: Whether the result is expected to be a JSON object that should be sent by value.

parameters.name: generatePreview
parameters.description: Whether preview should be generated for the result.

parameters.name: userGesture
parameters.description: Whether execution should be treated as initiated by user in the UI.

parameters.name: awaitPromise
parameters.description:
Whether execution should `await` for resulting value and return once awaited promise is
resolved.

parameters.name: throwOnSideEffect
parameters.description:
Whether to throw an exception if side effect cannot be ruled out during evaluation.
This implies `disableBreaks` below.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Terminate execution after timing out (number of milliseconds).

parameters.name: disableBreaks
parameters.description: Disable breakpoints during execution.

parameters.name: replMode
parameters.description:
Setting this flag to true enables `let` re-declaration and top-level `await`.
Note that `let` variables can only be re-declared if they originate from
`replMode` themselves.

parameters.name: allowUnsafeEvalBlockedByCSP
parameters.description:
The Content Security Policy (CSP) for the target might block 'unsafe-eval'
which includes eval(), Function(), setTimeout() and setInterval()
when called with non-callable arguments. This flag bypasses CSP for this
evaluation and allows unsafe-eval. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: uniqueContextId
parameters.description:
An alternative way to specify the execution context to evaluate in.
Compared to contextId that may be reused across processes, this is guaranteed to be
system-unique, so it can be used to prevent accidental evaluation of the expression
in context different than intended (e.g. as a result of navigation across process
boundaries).
This is mutually exclusive with `contextId`.

parameters.name: serializationOptions
parameters.description:
Specifies the result serialization. If provided, overrides
`generatePreview` and `returnByValue`.

returns.name: result
returns.description: Evaluation result.

returns.name: exceptionDetails
returns.description: Exception details.

commands.name: getExceptionDetails
commands.description:
This method tries to lookup and populate exception details for a
JavaScript Error object.
Note that the stackTrace portion of the resulting exceptionDetails will
only be populated if the Runtime domain was enabled at the time when the
Error was thrown.

parameters.name: errorObjectId
parameters.description: The error object for which to resolve the exception details.

commands.name: getHeapUsage
commands.description:
Returns the JavaScript heap usage.
It is the total usage of the corresponding isolate not scoped to a particular Runtime.

returns.name: usedSize
returns.description: Used JavaScript heap size in bytes.

returns.name: totalSize
returns.description: Allocated JavaScript heap size in bytes.

returns.name: embedderHeapUsedSize
returns.description: Used size in bytes in the embedder's garbage-collected heap.

returns.name: backingStorageSize
returns.description: Size in bytes of backing storage for array buffers and external strings.

commands.name: getIsolateId
commands.description: Returns the isolate id.

returns.name: id
returns.description: The isolate id.

commands.name: getProperties
commands.description:
Returns properties of a given object. Object group of the result is inherited from the target
object.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: Identifier of the object to return properties for.

parameters.name: ownProperties
parameters.description:
If true, returns properties belonging only to the element itself, not to its prototype
chain.

parameters.name: accessorPropertiesOnly
parameters.description:
If true, returns accessor properties (with getter/setter) only; internal properties are not
returned either.

parameters.name: generatePreview
parameters.description: Whether preview should be generated for the results.

parameters.name: nonIndexedPropertiesOnly
parameters.description: If true, returns non-indexed properties only.

returns.name: result
returns.description: Object properties.

returns.name: internalProperties
returns.description: Internal object properties (only of the element itself).

returns.name: privateProperties
returns.description: Object private properties.

returns.name: exceptionDetails
returns.description: Exception details.

commands.name: globalLexicalScopeNames
commands.description: Returns all let, const and class variables from global scope.

parameters.name: executionContextId
parameters.description: Specifies in which execution context to lookup global scope variables.

parameters.name: prototypeObjectId
parameters.description: Identifier of the prototype to return objects for.

parameters.name: objectGroup
parameters.description: Symbolic group name that can be used to release the results.

returns.name: objects
returns.description: Array with objects.

commands.name: releaseObject
commands.description: Releases remote object with given id.

parameters.name: objectId
parameters.description: Identifier of the object to release.

commands.name: releaseObjectGroup
commands.description: Releases all remote objects that belong to a given group.

parameters.name: objectGroup
parameters.description: Symbolic object group name.

commands.name: removeBinding
commands.description:
This method does not remove binding function from global object but
unsubscribes current runtime agent from Runtime.bindingCalled notifications.

commands.name: runIfWaitingForDebugger
commands.description: Tells inspected instance to run if it was waiting for debugger to attach.

commands.name: runScript
commands.description: Runs script with given id in a given context.

parameters.name: scriptId
parameters.description: Id of the script to run.

parameters.name: executionContextId
parameters.description:
Specifies in which execution context to perform script run. If the parameter is omitted the
evaluation will be performed in the context of the inspected page.

parameters.name: objectGroup
parameters.description: Symbolic group name that can be used to release multiple objects.

parameters.name: silent
parameters.description:
In silent mode exceptions thrown during evaluation are not reported and do not pause
execution. Overrides `setPauseOnException` state.

parameters.name: includeCommandLineAPI
parameters.description: Determines whether Command Line API should be available during the evaluation.

parameters.name: returnByValue
parameters.description: Whether the result is expected to be a JSON object which should be sent by value.

parameters.name: generatePreview
parameters.description: Whether preview should be generated for the result.

parameters.name: awaitPromise
parameters.description:
Whether execution should `await` for resulting value and return once awaited promise is
resolved.

returns.name: result
returns.description: Run result.

returns.name: exceptionDetails
returns.description: Exception details.

commands.name: setAsyncCallStackDepth
commands.description: Enables or disables async call stacks tracking.

parameters.name: maxDepth
parameters.description:
Maximum depth of async call stacks. Setting to `0` will effectively disable collecting async
call stacks (default).

commands.name: terminateExecution
commands.description:
Terminate current or next JavaScript execution.
Will cancel the termination when the outer-most script execution ends.

events.name: bindingCalled
events.description: Notification is issued every time when binding is called.

parameters.name: executionContextId
parameters.description: Identifier of the context where the call was made.

events.name: consoleAPICalled
events.description: Issued when console API was called.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of the call.

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Call arguments.

parameters.name: executionContextId
parameters.description: Identifier of the context where the call was made.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Call timestamp.

parameters.name: stackTrace
parameters.description:
Stack trace captured when the call was made. The async stack chain is automatically reported for
the following call types: `assert`, `error`, `trace`, `warning`. For other types the async call
chain can be retrieved using `Debugger.getStackTrace` and `stackTrace.parentId` field.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description:
Console context descriptor for calls on non-default console context (not console.*):
'anonymous#unique-logger-id' for call on unnamed context, 'name#unique-logger-id' for call
on named context.

events.name: exceptionRevoked
events.description: Issued when unhandled exception was revoked.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Reason describing why exception was revoked.

parameters.name: exceptionId
parameters.description: The id of revoked exception, as reported in `exceptionThrown`.

events.name: exceptionThrown
events.description: Issued when exception was thrown and unhandled.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp of the exception.

events.name: executionContextCreated
events.description: Issued when new execution context is created.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: A newly created execution context.

events.name: executionContextDestroyed
events.description: Issued when execution context is destroyed.

parameters.name: executionContextId
parameters.description: Id of the destroyed context

parameters.name: executionContextUniqueId
parameters.description: Unique Id of the destroyed context

events.name: executionContextsCleared
events.description: Issued when all executionContexts were cleared in browser

events.name: inspectRequested
events.description:
Issued when object should be inspected (for example, as a result of inspect() command line API
call).

parameters.name: executionContextId
parameters.description: Identifier of the context where the call was made.

types.name: Runtime.CallArgument
types.description:
Represents function call argument. Either remote object id `objectId`, primitive `value`,
unserializable primitive value or neither of (for undefined) them should be specified.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Primitive value or serializable javascript object.

properties.name: unserializableValue
properties.description: Primitive value which can not be JSON-stringified.

properties.name: objectId
properties.description: Remote object handle.

types.name: Runtime.CallFrame
types.description: Stack entry for runtime errors and assertions.

properties.name: functionName
properties.description: JavaScript function name.

properties.name: scriptId
properties.description: JavaScript script id.

properties.name: url
properties.description: JavaScript script name or url.

properties.name: lineNumber
properties.description: JavaScript script line number (0-based).

properties.name: columnNumber
properties.description: JavaScript script column number (0-based).

properties.name: header
properties.description:
The JSON-stringified result of formatter.header(object, config) call.
It contains json ML array that represents RemoteObject.

properties.name: bodyGetterId
properties.description:
If formatter returns true as a result of formatter.hasBody call then bodyGetterId will
contain RemoteObjectId for the function that returns result of formatter.body(object, config) call.
The result value is json ML array.

types.name: Runtime.DeepSerializedValue
types.description: Represents deep serialized value.

properties.name: weakLocalObjectReference
properties.description:
Set if value reference met more then once during serialization. In such
case, value is provided only to one of the serialized values. Unique
per value in the scope of one CDP call.

properties.name: key
properties.description: Preview of the key. Specified for map-like collection entries.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Preview of the value.

types.name: Runtime.ExceptionDetails
types.description:
Detailed information about exception (or error) that was thrown during script compilation or
execution.

properties.name: exceptionId
properties.description: Exception id.

properties.name: text
properties.description: Exception text, which should be used together with exception object when available.

properties.name: lineNumber
properties.description: Line number of the exception location (0-based).

properties.name: columnNumber
properties.description: Column number of the exception location (0-based).

properties.name: scriptId
properties.description: Script ID of the exception location.

properties.name: url
properties.description: URL of the exception location, to be used when the script was not reported.

properties.name: stackTrace
properties.description: JavaScript stack trace if available.

properties.name: exception
properties.description: Exception object if available.

properties.name: executionContextId
properties.description: Identifier of the context where exception happened.

properties.name: exceptionMetaData
properties.description:
Dictionary with entries of meta data that the client associated
with this exception, such as information about associated network
requests, etc.

types.name: Runtime.ExecutionContextDescription
types.description: Description of an isolated world.

properties.name: id
properties.description:
Unique id of the execution context. It can be used to specify in which execution context
script evaluation should be performed.

properties.name: origin
properties.description: Execution context origin.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Human readable name describing given context.

properties.name: uniqueId
properties.description:
A system-unique execution context identifier. Unlike the id, this is unique across
multiple processes, so can be reliably used to identify specific context while backend
performs a cross-process navigation.

properties.name: auxData
properties.description: Embedder-specific auxiliary data likely matching {isDefault: boolean, type: 'default'|'isolated'|'worker', frameId: string}

types.name: Runtime.ExecutionContextId
types.description: Id of an execution context.

types.name: Runtime.InternalPropertyDescriptor
types.description: Object internal property descriptor. This property isn't normally visible in JavaScript code.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Conventional property name.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The value associated with the property.

types.name: Runtime.ObjectPreview
types.description: Object containing abbreviated remote object value.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Object type.

properties.name: subtype
properties.description: Object subtype hint. Specified for `object` type values only.

properties.name: description
properties.description: String representation of the object.

properties.name: overflow
properties.description: True iff some of the properties or entries of the original object did not fit.

properties.name: properties
properties.description: List of the properties.

properties.name: entries
properties.description: List of the entries. Specified for `map` and `set` subtype values only.

types.name: Runtime.PrivatePropertyDescriptor
types.description: Object private field descriptor.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Private property name.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The value associated with the private property.

properties.name: get
properties.description:
A function which serves as a getter for the private property,
or `undefined` if there is no getter (accessor descriptors only).

properties.name: set
properties.description:
A function which serves as a setter for the private property,
or `undefined` if there is no setter (accessor descriptors only).

types.name: Runtime.PropertyDescriptor
types.description: Object property descriptor.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Property name or symbol description.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The value associated with the property.

properties.name: writable
properties.description: True if the value associated with the property may be changed (data descriptors only).

properties.name: get
properties.description:
A function which serves as a getter for the property, or `undefined` if there is no getter
(accessor descriptors only).

properties.name: set
properties.description:
A function which serves as a setter for the property, or `undefined` if there is no setter
(accessor descriptors only).

properties.name: configurable
properties.description:
True if the type of this property descriptor may be changed and if the property may be
deleted from the corresponding object.

properties.name: enumerable
properties.description:
True if this property shows up during enumeration of the properties on the corresponding
object.

properties.name: wasThrown
properties.description: True if the result was thrown during the evaluation.

properties.name: isOwn
properties.description: True if the property is owned for the object.

properties.name: symbol
properties.description: Property symbol object, if the property is of the `symbol` type.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Property name.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Object type. Accessor means that the property itself is an accessor property.

properties.name: value
properties.description: User-friendly property value string.

properties.name: valuePreview
properties.description: Nested value preview.

properties.name: subtype
properties.description: Object subtype hint. Specified for `object` type values only.

types.name: Runtime.RemoteObject
types.description: Mirror object referencing original JavaScript object.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Object type.

properties.name: subtype
properties.description:
Object subtype hint. Specified for `object` type values only.
NOTE: If you change anything here, make sure to also update
`subtype` in `ObjectPreview` and `PropertyPreview` below.

properties.name: className
properties.description: Object class (constructor) name. Specified for `object` type values only.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Remote object value in case of primitive values or JSON values (if it was requested).

properties.name: unserializableValue
properties.description:
Primitive value which can not be JSON-stringified does not have `value`, but gets this
property.

properties.name: description
properties.description: String representation of the object.

properties.name: deepSerializedValue
properties.description: Deep serialized value.

properties.name: objectId
properties.description: Unique object identifier (for non-primitive values).

properties.name: preview
properties.description: Preview containing abbreviated property values. Specified for `object` type values only.

types.name: Runtime.RemoteObjectId
types.description: Unique object identifier.

types.name: Runtime.ScriptId
types.description: Unique script identifier.

types.name: Runtime.SerializationOptions
types.description: Represents options for serialization. Overrides `generatePreview` and `returnByValue`.

properties.name: maxDepth
properties.description: Deep serialization depth. Default is full depth. Respected only in `deep` serialization mode.

properties.name: additionalParameters
properties.description:
Embedder-specific parameters. For example if connected to V8 in Chrome these control DOM
serialization via `maxNodeDepth: integer` and `includeShadowTree: "none" | "open" | "all"`.
Values can be only of type string or integer.

types.name: Runtime.StackTrace
types.description: Call frames for assertions or error messages.

properties.name: description
properties.description:
String label of this stack trace. For async traces this may be a name of the function that
initiated the async call.

properties.name: callFrames
properties.description: JavaScript function name.

properties.name: parent
properties.description: Asynchronous JavaScript stack trace that preceded this stack, if available.

properties.name: parentId
properties.description: Asynchronous JavaScript stack trace that preceded this stack, if available.

types.name: Runtime.StackTraceId
types.description:
If `debuggerId` is set stack trace comes from another debugger and can be resolved there. This
allows to track cross-debugger calls. See `Runtime.StackTrace` and `Debugger.paused` for usages.

types.name: Runtime.TimeDelta
types.description: Number of milliseconds.

types.name: Runtime.Timestamp
types.description: Number of milliseconds since epoch.

types.name: Runtime.UniqueDebuggerId
types.description: Unique identifier of current debugger.

types.name: Runtime.UnserializableValue
types.description:
Primitive value which cannot be JSON-stringified. Includes values `-0`, `NaN`, `Infinity`,
`-Infinity`, and bigint literals.

domains.name: Schema
domains.description: This domain is deprecated.

commands.name: getDomains
commands.description: Returns supported domains.

returns.name: domains
returns.description: List of supported domains.

types.name: Schema.Domain
types.description: Description of the protocol domain.

properties.name: name

properties.name: version
properties.description: Domain version.

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables tracking security state changes.

commands.name: enable
commands.description: Enables tracking security state changes.

commands.name: handleCertificateError
commands.description: Handles a certificate error that fired a certificateError event.

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: The ID of the event.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The action to take on the certificate error.

commands.name: setIgnoreCertificateErrors
commands.description: Enable/disable whether all certificate errors should be ignored.

parameters.name: ignore
parameters.description: If true, all certificate errors will be ignored.

commands.name: setOverrideCertificateErrors
commands.description:
Enable/disable overriding certificate errors. If enabled, all certificate error events need to
be handled by the DevTools client and should be answered with `handleCertificateError` commands.

parameters.name: override
parameters.description: If true, certificate errors will be overridden.

events.name: certificateError
events.description:
There is a certificate error. If overriding certificate errors is enabled, then it should be
handled with the `handleCertificateError` command. Note: this event does not fire if the
certificate error has been allowed internally. Only one client per target should override
certificate errors at the same time.

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: The ID of the event.

parameters.name: errorType
parameters.description: The type of the error.

parameters.name: requestURL
parameters.description: The url that was requested.

events.name: securityStateChanged
events.description: The security state of the page changed. No longer being sent.

parameters.name: securityState
parameters.description: Security state.

parameters.name: schemeIsCryptographic
parameters.description: True if the page was loaded over cryptographic transport such as HTTPS.

parameters.name: explanations
parameters.description:
Previously a list of explanations for the security state. Now always
empty.

parameters.name: insecureContentStatus
parameters.description: Information about insecure content on the page.

parameters.name: summary
parameters.description: Overrides user-visible description of the state. Always omitted.

events.name: visibleSecurityStateChanged
events.description: The security state of the page changed.

parameters.name: visibleSecurityState
parameters.description: Security state information about the page.

types.name: Security.CertificateErrorAction

types.name: Security.CertificateId

Removes all Trust Tokens issued by the provided issuerOrigin.
Leaves other stored data, including the issuer's Redemption Records, intact.

returns.description: True if any tokens were deleted, false otherwise.

This largely corresponds to:
https://wicg.github.io/turtledove/#dictdef-generatebidinterestgroup
but has absolute expirationTime instead of relative lifetimeMs and
also adds joiningOrigin.

commands.description:
Returns the effective Related Website Sets in use by this profile for the browser
session. The effective Related Website Sets will not change during a browser session.

commands.name: getSharedStorageMetadata
commands.description: Gets metadata for an origin's shared storage.

commands.description:
Returns storage key for the given frame. If no frame ID is provided,
the storage key of the target executing this command is returned.

commands.name: getStorageKeyForFrame
commands.description:
Returns a storage key given a frame id.
Deprecated. Please use Storage.getStorageKey instead.

commands.name: getTrustTokens

Returns the number of stored Trust Tokens per issuer for the
current browsing context.

commands.name: getUsageAndQuota
commands.description: Returns usage and quota in bytes.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: Security origin.

returns.name: usage
returns.description: Storage usage (bytes).

returns.name: quota
returns.description: Storage quota (bytes).

returns.name: overrideActive
returns.description: Whether or not the origin has an active storage quota override

returns.name: usageBreakdown
returns.description: Storage usage per type (bytes).

commands.name: overrideQuotaForOrigin
commands.description: Override quota for the specified origin

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description: Security origin.

parameters.name: quotaSize
parameters.description:
The quota size (in bytes) to override the original quota with.
If this is called multiple times, the overridden quota will be equal to
the quotaSize provided in the final call. If this is called without
specifying a quotaSize, the quota will be reset to the default value for
the specified origin. If this is called multiple times with different
origins, the override will be maintained for each origin until it is
disabled (called without a quotaSize).

commands.description: Resets the budget for `ownerOrigin` by clearing all budget withdrawals.

events.name: interestGroupAccessed
events.description:
One of the interest groups was accessed. Note that these events are global
to all targets sharing an interest group store.

parameters.name: componentSellerOrigin
parameters.description:
For topLevelBid/topLevelAdditionalBid, and when appropriate,
win and additionalBidWin

parameters.name: bid
parameters.description: For bid or somethingBid event, if done locally and not on a server.

parameters.name: uniqueAuctionId
parameters.description: For non-global events --- links to interestGroupAuctionEvent

events.name: interestGroupAuctionEventOccurred
events.description:
An auction involving interest groups is taking place. These events are
target-specific.

parameters.name: parentAuctionId
parameters.description: Set for child auctions.

parameters.name: auctionConfig
parameters.description: Set for started and configResolved

events.name: interestGroupAuctionNetworkRequestCreated
events.description:
Specifies which auctions a particular network fetch may be related to, and
in what role. Note that it is not ordered with respect to
Network.requestWillBeSent (but will happen before loadingFinished
loadingFailed).

parameters.name: auctions
parameters.description:
This is the set of the auctions using the worklet that issued this
request.  In the case of trusted signals, it's possible that only some of
them actually care about the keys being queried.

events.name: sharedStorageAccessed
events.description:
Shared storage was accessed by the associated page.
The following parameters are included in all events.

parameters.name: accessTime
parameters.description: Time of the access.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Enum value indicating the access scope.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: Enum value indicating the Shared Storage API method invoked.

parameters.name: mainFrameId
parameters.description: DevTools Frame Token for the primary frame tree's root.

parameters.name: ownerOrigin
parameters.description: Serialization of the origin owning the Shared Storage data.

parameters.name: ownerSite
parameters.description: Serialization of the site owning the Shared Storage data.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description:
The sub-parameters wrapped by `params` are all optional and their
presence/absence depends on `type`.

events.name: sharedStorageWorkletOperationExecutionFinished
events.description:
A shared storage run or selectURL operation finished its execution.
The following parameters are included in all events.

parameters.name: finishedTime
parameters.description: Time that the operation finished.

parameters.name: executionTime
parameters.description:
Time, in microseconds, from start of shared storage JS API call until
end of operation execution in the worklet.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: Enum value indicating the Shared Storage API method invoked.

parameters.name: operationId
parameters.description: ID of the operation call.

parameters.name: workletTargetId
parameters.description:
Hex representation of the DevTools token used as the TargetID for the
associated shared storage worklet.

parameters.name: mainFrameId
parameters.description: DevTools Frame Token for the primary frame tree's root.

parameters.name: ownerOrigin
parameters.description: Serialization of the origin owning the Shared Storage data.

types.description: Protected audience interest group auction identifier.

types.name: Storage.RelatedWebsiteSet
types.description: A single Related Website Set object.

properties.name: primarySites
properties.description: The primary site of this set, along with the ccTLDs if there is any.

properties.name: associatedSites
properties.description: The associated sites of this set, along with the ccTLDs if there is any.

properties.name: serviceSites
properties.description: The service sites of this set, along with the ccTLDs if there is any.

types.name: Storage.SharedStorageAccessMethod
types.description: Enum of shared storage access methods.

types.name: Storage.SharedStorageAccessParams
types.description:
Bundles the parameters for shared storage access events whose
presence/absence can vary according to SharedStorageAccessType.

properties.name: scriptSourceUrl
properties.description:
Spec of the module script URL.
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethods: addModule and
createWorklet.

properties.name: dataOrigin
properties.description:
String denoting "context-origin", "script-origin", or a custom
origin to be used as the worklet's data origin.
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethod: createWorklet.

properties.name: operationName
properties.description:
Name of the registered operation to be run.
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethods: run and selectURL.

properties.name: operationId
properties.description:
ID of the operation call.
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethods: run and selectURL.

properties.name: keepAlive
properties.description:
Whether or not to keep the worket alive for future run or selectURL
calls.
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethods: run and selectURL.

properties.name: privateAggregationConfig
properties.description:
Configures the private aggregation options.
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethods: run and selectURL.

properties.name: serializedData
properties.description:
The operation's serialized data in bytes (converted to a string).
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethods: run and selectURL.
TODO(crbug.com/401011862): Consider updating this parameter to binary.

properties.name: urlsWithMetadata
properties.description:
Array of candidate URLs' specs, along with any associated metadata.
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethod: selectURL.

properties.name: urnUuid
properties.description:
Spec of the URN:UUID generated for a selectURL call.
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethod: selectURL.

properties.name: key
properties.description:
Key for a specific entry in an origin's shared storage.
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethods: set, append, delete, and
get.

properties.name: value
properties.description:
Value for a specific entry in an origin's shared storage.
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethods: set and append.

properties.name: ignoreIfPresent
properties.description:
Whether or not to set an entry for a key if that key is already present.
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethod: set.

properties.name: workletOrdinal
properties.description:
A number denoting the (0-based) order of the worklet's
creation relative to all other shared storage worklets created by
documents using the current storage partition.
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethods: addModule, createWorklet.

properties.name: workletTargetId
properties.description:
Hex representation of the DevTools token used as the TargetID for the
associated shared storage worklet.
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethods: addModule, createWorklet,
run, selectURL, and any other SharedStorageAccessMethod when the
SharedStorageAccessScope is sharedStorageWorklet.

properties.name: withLock
properties.description:
Name of the lock to be acquired, if present.
Optionally present only for SharedStorageAccessMethods: batchUpdate,
set, append, delete, and clear.

properties.name: batchUpdateId
properties.description:
If the method has been called as part of a batchUpdate, then this
number identifies the batch to which it belongs.
Optionally present only for SharedStorageAccessMethods:
batchUpdate (required), set, append, delete, and clear.

properties.name: batchSize
properties.description:
Number of modifier methods sent in batch.
Present only for SharedStorageAccessMethod: batchUpdate.

domains.description: The SystemInfo domain defines methods and events for querying low-level system information.

commands.name: getFeatureState
