returns.description:
A platform-dependent description of the model of the machine. On Mac OS, this is, for
example, 'MacBookPro'. Will be the empty string if not supported.

returns.name: modelVersion
returns.description:
A platform-dependent description of the version of the machine. On Mac OS, this is, for
example, '10.1'. Will be the empty string if not supported.

returns.name: commandLine
returns.description:
The command line string used to launch the browser. Will be the empty string if not
supported.

commands.name: getProcessInfo
commands.description: Returns information about all running processes.

returns.name: processInfo
returns.description: An array of process info blocks.

types.name: SystemInfo.GPUDevice
types.description: Describes a single graphics processor (GPU).

properties.name: vendorId
properties.description: PCI ID of the GPU vendor, if available; 0 otherwise.

properties.name: deviceId
properties.description: PCI ID of the GPU device, if available; 0 otherwise.

properties.name: subSysId
properties.description: Sub sys ID of the GPU, only available on Windows.

properties.name: revision
properties.description: Revision of the GPU, only available on Windows.

properties.name: vendorString
properties.description: String description of the GPU vendor, if the PCI ID is not available.

properties.name: deviceString
properties.description: String description of the GPU device, if the PCI ID is not available.

properties.name: driverVendor
properties.description: String description of the GPU driver vendor.

properties.name: driverVersion
properties.description: String description of the GPU driver version.

types.name: SystemInfo.GPUInfo
types.description: Provides information about the GPU(s) on the system.

properties.name: devices
properties.description: The graphics devices on the system. Element 0 is the primary GPU.

properties.name: auxAttributes
properties.description: An optional dictionary of additional GPU related attributes.

properties.name: featureStatus
properties.description: An optional dictionary of graphics features and their status.

properties.name: driverBugWorkarounds
properties.description: An optional array of GPU driver bug workarounds.

properties.name: videoDecoding
properties.description: Supported accelerated video decoding capabilities.

properties.name: videoEncoding
properties.description: Supported accelerated video encoding capabilities.

types.name: SystemInfo.ImageType
types.description: Image format of a given image.

types.name: SystemInfo.ProcessInfo
types.description: Represents process info.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Specifies process type.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Specifies process id.

properties.name: cpuTime
properties.description:
Specifies cumulative CPU usage in seconds across all threads of the
process since the process start.

types.name: SystemInfo.Size
types.description: Describes the width and height dimensions of an entity.

properties.name: width
properties.description: Width in pixels.

properties.name: height
properties.description: Height in pixels.

types.name: SystemInfo.SubsamplingFormat
types.description: YUV subsampling type of the pixels of a given image.

types.name: SystemInfo.VideoDecodeAcceleratorCapability
types.description:
Describes a supported video decoding profile with its associated minimum and
maximum resolutions.

properties.name: profile
properties.description: Video codec profile that is supported, e.g. VP9 Profile 2.

properties.name: maxResolution
properties.description: Maximum video dimensions in pixels supported for this |profile|.

properties.name: minResolution
properties.description: Minimum video dimensions in pixels supported for this |profile|.

types.name: SystemInfo.VideoEncodeAcceleratorCapability
types.description:
Describes a supported video encoding profile with its associated maximum
resolution and maximum framerate.

properties.name: profile
properties.description: Video codec profile that is supported, e.g H264 Main.

properties.name: maxResolution
properties.description: Maximum video dimensions in pixels supported for this |profile|.

properties.name: maxFramerateNumerator
properties.description:
Maximum encoding framerate in frames per second supported for this
|profile|, as fraction's numerator and denominator, e.g. 24/1 fps,
24000/1001 fps, etc.

domains.name: Target
domains.description: Supports additional targets discovery and allows to attach to them.

commands.name: activateTarget
commands.description: Activates (focuses) the target.

commands.name: attachToBrowserTarget
commands.description: Attaches to the browser target, only uses flat sessionId mode.

returns.name: sessionId
returns.description: Id assigned to the session.

commands.name: attachToTarget
commands.description: Attaches to the target with given id.

parameters.name: flatten
parameters.description:
Enables "flat" access to the session via specifying sessionId attribute in the commands.
We plan to make this the default, deprecate non-flattened mode,
and eventually retire it. See crbug.com/991325.

returns.name: sessionId
returns.description: Id assigned to the session.

commands.name: autoAttachRelated
commands.description:
Adds the specified target to the list of targets that will be monitored for any related target
creation (such as child frames, child workers and new versions of service worker) and reported
through `attachedToTarget`. The specified target is also auto-attached.
This cancels the effect of any previous `setAutoAttach` and is also cancelled by subsequent
`setAutoAttach`. Only available at the Browser target.

parameters.name: waitForDebuggerOnStart
parameters.description:
Whether to pause new targets when attaching to them. Use `Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger`
to run paused targets.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Only targets matching filter will be attached.

commands.name: closeTarget
commands.description: Closes the target. If the target is a page that gets closed too.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Always set to true. If an error occurs, the response indicates protocol error.

commands.name: createBrowserContext
commands.description:
Creates a new empty BrowserContext. Similar to an incognito profile but you can have more than
one.

parameters.name: disposeOnDetach
parameters.description: If specified, disposes this context when debugging session disconnects.

parameters.name: proxyServer
parameters.description: Proxy server, similar to the one passed to --proxy-server

parameters.name: proxyBypassList
parameters.description: Proxy bypass list, similar to the one passed to --proxy-bypass-list

parameters.name: originsWithUniversalNetworkAccess
parameters.description:
An optional list of origins to grant unlimited cross-origin access to.
Parts of the URL other than those constituting origin are ignored.

returns.name: browserContextId
returns.description: The id of the context created.

commands.name: createTarget
commands.description: Creates a new page.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The initial URL the page will be navigated to. An empty string indicates about:blank.

parameters.name: left
parameters.description: Frame left origin in DIP (requires newWindow to be true or headless shell).

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Frame top origin in DIP (requires newWindow to be true or headless shell).

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: Frame width in DIP (requires newWindow to be true or headless shell).

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: Frame height in DIP (requires newWindow to be true or headless shell).

parameters.name: windowState
parameters.description:
Frame window state (requires newWindow to be true or headless shell).
Default is normal.

parameters.name: browserContextId
parameters.description: The browser context to create the page in.

parameters.name: enableBeginFrameControl
parameters.description:
Whether BeginFrames for this target will be controlled via DevTools (headless shell only,
not supported on MacOS yet, false by default).

parameters.name: newWindow
parameters.description: Whether to create a new Window or Tab (false by default, not supported by headless shell).

parameters.name: background
parameters.description:
Whether to create the target in background or foreground (false by default, not supported
by headless shell).

parameters.name: forTab
parameters.description: Whether to create the target of type "tab".

parameters.name: hidden
parameters.description:
Whether to create a hidden target. The hidden target is observable via protocol, but not
present in the tab UI strip. Cannot be created with `forTab: true`, `newWindow: true` or
`background: false`. The life-time of the tab is limited to the life-time of the session.

parameters.name: focus
parameters.description:
If specified, the option is used to determine if the new target should
be focused or not. By default, the focus behavior depends on the
value of the background field. For example, background=false and focus=false
will result in the target tab being opened but the browser window remain
unchanged (if it was in the background, it will remain in the background)
and background=false with focus=undefined will result in the window being focused.
Using background: true and focus: true is not supported and will result in an error.

returns.name: targetId
returns.description: The id of the page opened.

commands.name: detachFromTarget
commands.description: Detaches session with given id.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Session to detach.

parameters.name: targetId
parameters.description: Deprecated.

commands.name: disposeBrowserContext
commands.description:
Deletes a BrowserContext. All the belonging pages will be closed without calling their
beforeunload hooks.

commands.name: exposeDevToolsProtocol
commands.description:
Inject object to the target's main frame that provides a communication
channel with browser target.

Injected object will be available as `window[bindingName]`.

The object has the following API:
- `binding.send(json)` - a method to send messages over the remote debugging protocol
- `binding.onmessage = json => handleMessage(json)` - a callback that will be called for the protocol notifications and command responses.

parameters.name: bindingName
parameters.description: Binding name, 'cdp' if not specified.

parameters.name: inheritPermissions
parameters.description: If true, inherits the current root session's permissions (default: false).

commands.name: getBrowserContexts
commands.description: Returns all browser contexts created with `Target.createBrowserContext` method.

returns.name: browserContextIds
returns.description: An array of browser context ids.

returns.name: defaultBrowserContextId
returns.description: The id of the default browser context if available.

commands.name: getDevToolsTarget
commands.description:
Gets the targetId of the DevTools page target opened for the given target
(if any).

parameters.name: targetId
parameters.description: Page or tab target ID.

returns.name: targetId
returns.description: The targetId of DevTools page target if exists.

commands.name: getTargetInfo
commands.description: Returns information about a target.

commands.name: getTargets
commands.description: Retrieves a list of available targets.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Only targets matching filter will be reported. If filter is not specified
and target discovery is currently enabled, a filter used for target discovery
is used for consistency.

returns.name: targetInfos
returns.description: The list of targets.

commands.name: openDevTools
commands.description: Opens a DevTools window for the target.

parameters.name: targetId
parameters.description: This can be the page or tab target ID.

parameters.name: panelId
parameters.description:
The id of the panel we want DevTools to open initially. Currently
supported panels are elements, console, network, sources, resources
and performance.

returns.name: targetId
returns.description: The targetId of DevTools page target.

commands.name: sendMessageToTarget
commands.description:
Sends protocol message over session with given id.
Consider using flat mode instead; see commands attachToTarget, setAutoAttach,
and crbug.com/991325.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Identifier of the session.

parameters.name: targetId
parameters.description: Deprecated.

commands.name: setAutoAttach
commands.description:
Controls whether to automatically attach to new targets which are considered
to be directly related to this one (for example, iframes or workers).
When turned on, attaches to all existing related targets as well. When turned off,
automatically detaches from all currently attached targets.
This also clears all targets added by `autoAttachRelated` from the list of targets to watch
for creation of related targets.
You might want to call this recursively for auto-attached targets to attach
to all available targets.

parameters.name: autoAttach
parameters.description: Whether to auto-attach to related targets.

parameters.name: waitForDebuggerOnStart
parameters.description:
Whether to pause new targets when attaching to them. Use `Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger`
to run paused targets.

parameters.name: flatten
parameters.description:
Enables "flat" access to the session via specifying sessionId attribute in the commands.
We plan to make this the default, deprecate non-flattened mode,
and eventually retire it. See crbug.com/991325.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Only targets matching filter will be attached.

commands.name: setDiscoverTargets
commands.description:
Controls whether to discover available targets and notify via
`targetCreated/targetInfoChanged/targetDestroyed` events.

parameters.name: discover
parameters.description: Whether to discover available targets.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Only targets matching filter will be attached. If `discover` is false,
`filter` must be omitted or empty.

commands.name: setRemoteLocations
commands.description:
Enables target discovery for the specified locations, when `setDiscoverTargets` was set to
`true`.

parameters.name: locations
parameters.description: List of remote locations.

events.name: attachedToTarget
events.description: Issued when attached to target because of auto-attach or `attachToTarget` command.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Identifier assigned to the session used to send/receive messages.

events.name: detachedFromTarget
events.description:
Issued when detached from target for any reason (including `detachFromTarget` command). Can be
issued multiple times per target if multiple sessions have been attached to it.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Detached session identifier.

parameters.name: targetId
parameters.description: Deprecated.

events.name: receivedMessageFromTarget
events.description:
Notifies about a new protocol message received from the session (as reported in
`attachedToTarget` event).

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Identifier of a session which sends a message.

parameters.name: targetId
parameters.description: Deprecated.

events.name: targetCrashed
events.description: Issued when a target has crashed.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Termination status type.

parameters.name: errorCode
parameters.description: Termination error code.

events.name: targetCreated
events.description: Issued when a possible inspection target is created.

events.name: targetDestroyed
events.description: Issued when a target is destroyed.

events.name: targetInfoChanged
events.description:
Issued when some information about a target has changed. This only happens between
`targetCreated` and `targetDestroyed`.

types.name: Target.FilterEntry
types.description: A filter used by target query/discovery/auto-attach operations.

properties.name: exclude
properties.description: If set, causes exclusion of matching targets from the list.

properties.name: type
properties.description: If not present, matches any type.

types.name: Target.SessionID
types.description: Unique identifier of attached debugging session.

types.name: Target.TargetFilter
types.description:
The entries in TargetFilter are matched sequentially against targets and
the first entry that matches determines if the target is included or not,
depending on the value of `exclude` field in the entry.
If filter is not specified, the one assumed is
[{type: "browser", exclude: true}, {type: "tab", exclude: true}, {}]
(i.e. include everything but `browser` and `tab`).

properties.name: type
properties.description: List of types: https://source.chromium.org/chromium/chromium/src/+/main:content/browser/devtools/devtools_agent_host_impl.cc?ss=chromium&q=f:devtools%20-f:out%20%22::kTypeTab%5B%5D%22

properties.name: attached
properties.description: Whether the target has an attached client.

properties.name: openerId
properties.description: Opener target Id

properties.name: canAccessOpener
properties.description: Whether the target has access to the originating window.

properties.name: openerFrameId
properties.description: Frame id of originating window (is only set if target has an opener).

properties.name: parentFrameId
properties.description: Id of the parent frame, only present for the "iframe" targets.

properties.name: subtype
properties.description:
Provides additional details for specific target types. For example, for
the type of "page", this may be set to "prerender".

types.name: Target.WindowState
types.description: The state of the target window.

domains.name: Tethering
domains.description: The Tethering domain defines methods and events for browser port binding.

commands.name: bind
commands.description: Request browser port binding.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: Port number to bind.

commands.name: unbind
commands.description: Request browser port unbinding.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: Port number to unbind.

events.name: accepted
events.description: Informs that port was successfully bound and got a specified connection id.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: Port number that was successfully bound.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: Connection id to be used.

commands.name: end
commands.description: Stop trace events collection.

commands.name: getCategories
commands.description: Gets supported tracing categories.

returns.name: categories
returns.description: A list of supported tracing categories.

commands.name: getTrackEventDescriptor
commands.description: Return a descriptor for all available tracing categories.

returns.name: descriptor
returns.description: Base64-encoded serialized perfetto.protos.TrackEventDescriptor protobuf message. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

commands.name: recordClockSyncMarker
commands.description: Record a clock sync marker in the trace.

parameters.name: syncId
parameters.description: The ID of this clock sync marker

commands.name: requestMemoryDump
commands.description: Request a global memory dump.

parameters.name: deterministic
parameters.description: Enables more deterministic results by forcing garbage collection

parameters.name: levelOfDetail
parameters.description: Specifies level of details in memory dump. Defaults to "detailed".

returns.name: dumpGuid
returns.description: GUID of the resulting global memory dump.

returns.name: success
returns.description: True iff the global memory dump succeeded.

commands.name: start
commands.description: Start trace events collection.

parameters.name: categories
parameters.description: Category/tag filter

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: Tracing options

parameters.name: bufferUsageReportingInterval
parameters.description: If set, the agent will issue bufferUsage events at this interval, specified in milliseconds

parameters.name: transferMode
parameters.description:
Whether to report trace events as series of dataCollected events or to save trace to a
stream (defaults to `ReportEvents`).

parameters.name: streamFormat
parameters.description:
Trace data format to use. This only applies when using `ReturnAsStream`
transfer mode (defaults to `json`).

parameters.name: streamCompression
parameters.description:
Compression format to use. This only applies when using `ReturnAsStream`
transfer mode (defaults to `none`)

parameters.name: perfettoConfig
parameters.description:
Base64-encoded serialized perfetto.protos.TraceConfig protobuf message
When specified, the parameters `categories`, `options`, `traceConfig`
are ignored. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

parameters.name: tracingBackend
parameters.description: Backend type (defaults to `auto`)

parameters.name: percentFull
parameters.description:
A number in range [0..1] that indicates the used size of event buffer as a fraction of its
total size.

parameters.name: eventCount
parameters.description: An approximate number of events in the trace log.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description:
A number in range [0..1] that indicates the used size of event buffer as a fraction of its
total size.

events.name: dataCollected
events.description:
Contains a bucket of collected trace events. When tracing is stopped collected events will be
sent as a sequence of dataCollected events followed by tracingComplete event.

events.name: tracingComplete
events.description:
Signals that tracing is stopped and there is no trace buffers pending flush, all data were
delivered via dataCollected events.

parameters.name: dataLossOccurred
parameters.description:
Indicates whether some trace data is known to have been lost, e.g. because the trace ring
buffer wrapped around.

parameters.name: stream
parameters.description: A handle of the stream that holds resulting trace data.

parameters.name: traceFormat
parameters.description: Trace data format of returned stream.

parameters.name: streamCompression
parameters.description: Compression format of returned stream.

types.name: Tracing.MemoryDumpConfig
types.description: Configuration for memory dump. Used only when "memory-infra" category is enabled.

types.name: Tracing.MemoryDumpLevelOfDetail
types.description:
Details exposed when memory request explicitly declared.
Keep consistent with memory_dump_request_args.h and
memory_instrumentation.mojom

types.name: Tracing.StreamCompression
types.description: Compression type to use for traces returned via streams.

types.name: Tracing.StreamFormat
types.description:
Data format of a trace. Can be either the legacy JSON format or the
protocol buffer format. Note that the JSON format will be deprecated soon.

properties.name: recordMode
properties.description: Controls how the trace buffer stores data. The default is `recordUntilFull`.

properties.name: traceBufferSizeInKb
properties.description:
Size of the trace buffer in kilobytes. If not specified or zero is passed, a default value
of 200 MB would be used.

properties.name: enableSampling
properties.description: Turns on JavaScript stack sampling.

properties.name: enableSystrace
properties.description: Turns on system tracing.

properties.name: enableArgumentFilter
properties.description: Turns on argument filter.

properties.name: includedCategories
properties.description: Included category filters.

properties.name: excludedCategories
properties.description: Excluded category filters.

properties.name: syntheticDelays
properties.description: Configuration to synthesize the delays in tracing.

properties.name: memoryDumpConfig
properties.description: Configuration for memory dump triggers. Used only when "memory-infra" category is enabled.

types.name: Tracing.TracingBackend
types.description:
Backend type to use for tracing. `chrome` uses the Chrome-integrated
tracing service and is supported on all platforms. `system` is only
supported on Chrome OS and uses the Perfetto system tracing service.
`auto` chooses `system` when the perfettoConfig provided to Tracing.start
specifies at least one non-Chrome data source; otherwise uses `chrome`.

domains.name: WebAudio
domains.description:
This domain allows inspection of Web Audio API.
https://webaudio.github.io/web-audio-api/

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables the WebAudio domain.

commands.name: enable
commands.description: Enables the WebAudio domain and starts sending context lifetime events.

commands.name: getRealtimeData
commands.description: Fetch the realtime data from the registered contexts.

events.name: audioListenerCreated
events.description: Notifies that the construction of an AudioListener has finished.

events.name: audioListenerWillBeDestroyed
events.description: Notifies that a new AudioListener has been created.

events.name: audioNodeCreated
events.description: Notifies that a new AudioNode has been created.

events.name: audioNodeWillBeDestroyed
events.description: Notifies that an existing AudioNode has been destroyed.

events.name: audioParamCreated
events.description: Notifies that a new AudioParam has been created.

events.name: audioParamWillBeDestroyed
events.description: Notifies that an existing AudioParam has been destroyed.

events.name: contextChanged
events.description: Notifies that existing BaseAudioContext has changed some properties (id stays the same)..

events.name: contextCreated
events.description: Notifies that a new BaseAudioContext has been created.

events.name: contextWillBeDestroyed
events.description: Notifies that an existing BaseAudioContext will be destroyed.

events.name: nodeParamConnected
events.description: Notifies that an AudioNode is connected to an AudioParam.

events.name: nodeParamDisconnected
events.description: Notifies that an AudioNode is disconnected to an AudioParam.

events.name: nodesConnected
events.description: Notifies that two AudioNodes are connected.

events.name: nodesDisconnected
events.description: Notifies that AudioNodes are disconnected. The destination can be null, and it means all the outgoing connections from the source are disconnected.

types.name: WebAudio.AudioListener
types.description: Protocol object for AudioListener

types.name: WebAudio.AudioNode
types.description: Protocol object for AudioNode

types.name: WebAudio.AudioParam
types.description: Protocol object for AudioParam

types.name: WebAudio.AutomationRate
types.description: Enum of AudioParam::AutomationRate from the spec

types.name: WebAudio.BaseAudioContext
types.description: Protocol object for BaseAudioContext

properties.name: callbackBufferSize
properties.description: Platform-dependent callback buffer size.

properties.name: maxOutputChannelCount
properties.description: Number of output channels supported by audio hardware in use.

properties.name: sampleRate
properties.description: Context sample rate.

types.name: WebAudio.ChannelCountMode
types.description: Enum of AudioNode::ChannelCountMode from the spec

types.name: WebAudio.ChannelInterpretation
types.description: Enum of AudioNode::ChannelInterpretation from the spec

types.name: WebAudio.ContextRealtimeData
types.description: Fields in AudioContext that change in real-time.

properties.name: currentTime
properties.description: The current context time in second in BaseAudioContext.

properties.name: renderCapacity
properties.description:
The time spent on rendering graph divided by render quantum duration,
and multiplied by 100. 100 means the audio renderer reached the full
capacity and glitch may occur.

properties.name: callbackIntervalMean
properties.description: A running mean of callback interval.

properties.name: callbackIntervalVariance
properties.description: A running variance of callback interval.

types.name: WebAudio.ContextState
types.description: Enum of AudioContextState from the spec

types.name: WebAudio.ContextType
types.description: Enum of BaseAudioContext types

types.name: WebAudio.GraphObjectId
types.description: An unique ID for a graph object (AudioContext, AudioNode, AudioParam) in Web Audio API

types.name: WebAudio.NodeType
types.description: Enum of AudioNode types

types.name: WebAudio.ParamType
types.description: Enum of AudioParam types

domains.name: WebAuthn
domains.description:
This domain allows configuring virtual authenticators to test the WebAuthn
API.

commands.name: addCredential
commands.description: Adds the credential to the specified authenticator.

commands.name: addVirtualAuthenticator
commands.description: Creates and adds a virtual authenticator.

commands.name: clearCredentials
commands.description: Clears all the credentials from the specified device.

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disable the WebAuthn domain.

commands.name: enable
commands.description:
Enable the WebAuthn domain and start intercepting credential storage and
retrieval with a virtual authenticator.

parameters.name: enableUI
parameters.description:
Whether to enable the WebAuthn user interface. Enabling the UI is
recommended for debugging and demo purposes, as it is closer to the real
experience. Disabling the UI is recommended for automated testing.
Supported at the embedder's discretion if UI is available.
Defaults to false.

commands.name: getCredential
commands.description:
Returns a single credential stored in the given virtual authenticator that
matches the credential ID.

commands.name: getCredentials
commands.description: Returns all the credentials stored in the given virtual authenticator.

commands.name: removeCredential
commands.description: Removes a credential from the authenticator.

commands.name: removeVirtualAuthenticator
commands.description: Removes the given authenticator.

commands.name: setAutomaticPresenceSimulation
commands.description:
Sets whether tests of user presence will succeed immediately (if true) or fail to resolve (if false) for an authenticator.
The default is true.

commands.name: setCredentialProperties
commands.description:
Allows setting credential properties.
https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#sctn-automation-set-credential-properties

commands.name: setResponseOverrideBits
commands.description: Resets parameters isBogusSignature, isBadUV, isBadUP to false if they are not present.

parameters.name: isBogusSignature
parameters.description:
If isBogusSignature is set, overrides the signature in the authenticator response to be zero.
Defaults to false.

parameters.name: isBadUV
parameters.description:
If isBadUV is set, overrides the UV bit in the flags in the authenticator response to
be zero. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: isBadUP
parameters.description:
If isBadUP is set, overrides the UP bit in the flags in the authenticator response to
be zero. Defaults to false.

commands.name: setUserVerified
commands.description:
Sets whether User Verification succeeds or fails for an authenticator.
The default is true.

events.name: credentialAdded
events.description: Triggered when a credential is added to an authenticator.

events.name: credentialAsserted
events.description: Triggered when a credential is used in a webauthn assertion.

events.name: credentialDeleted
events.description:
Triggered when a credential is deleted, e.g. through
PublicKeyCredential.signalUnknownCredential().

events.name: credentialUpdated
events.description:
Triggered when a credential is updated, e.g. through
PublicKeyCredential.signalCurrentUserDetails().

properties.name: rpId
properties.description:
Relying Party ID the credential is scoped to. Must be set when adding a
credential.

properties.name: privateKey
properties.description: The ECDSA P-256 private key in PKCS#8 format. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

properties.name: userHandle
properties.description:
An opaque byte sequence with a maximum size of 64 bytes mapping the
credential to a specific user. (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

properties.name: signCount
properties.description:
Signature counter. This is incremented by one for each successful
assertion.
See https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#signature-counter

properties.name: largeBlob
properties.description:
The large blob associated with the credential.
See https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#sctn-large-blob-extension (Encoded as a base64 string when passed over JSON)

properties.name: backupEligibility
properties.description:
Assertions returned by this credential will have the backup eligibility
(BE) flag set to this value. Defaults to the authenticator's
defaultBackupEligibility value.

properties.name: backupState
properties.description:
Assertions returned by this credential will have the backup state (BS)
flag set to this value. Defaults to the authenticator's
defaultBackupState value.

properties.name: userName
properties.description:
The credential's user.name property. Equivalent to empty if not set.
https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#dom-publickeycredentialentity-name

properties.name: userDisplayName
properties.description:
The credential's user.displayName property. Equivalent to empty if
not set.
https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#dom-publickeycredentialuserentity-displayname

properties.name: ctap2Version
properties.description: Defaults to ctap2_0. Ignored if |protocol| == u2f.

properties.name: hasResidentKey
properties.description: Defaults to false.

properties.name: hasUserVerification
properties.description: Defaults to false.

properties.name: hasLargeBlob
properties.description:
If set to true, the authenticator will support the largeBlob extension.
https://w3c.github.io/webauthn#largeBlob
Defaults to false.

properties.name: hasCredBlob
properties.description:
If set to true, the authenticator will support the credBlob extension.
https://fidoalliance.org/specs/fido-v2.1-rd-20201208/fido-client-to-authenticator-protocol-v2.1-rd-20201208.html#sctn-credBlob-extension
Defaults to false.

properties.name: hasMinPinLength
properties.description:
If set to true, the authenticator will support the minPinLength extension.
https://fidoalliance.org/specs/fido-v2.1-ps-20210615/fido-client-to-authenticator-protocol-v2.1-ps-20210615.html#sctn-minpinlength-extension
Defaults to false.

properties.name: hasPrf
properties.description:
If set to true, the authenticator will support the prf extension.
https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#prf-extension
Defaults to false.

properties.name: hasHmacSecret
properties.description:
If set to true, the authenticator will support the hmac-secret extension.
https://fidoalliance.org/specs/fido-v2.1-ps-20210615/fido-client-to-authenticator-protocol-v2.1-ps-20210615.html#sctn-hmac-secret-extension
Defaults to false.

properties.name: hasHmacSecretMc
properties.description:
If set to true, the authenticator will support the hmac-secret-mc extension.
https://fidoalliance.org/specs/fido-v2.2-rd-20241003/fido-client-to-authenticator-protocol-v2.2-rd-20241003.html#sctn-hmac-secret-make-cred-extension
Defaults to false.

properties.name: automaticPresenceSimulation
properties.description:
If set to true, tests of user presence will succeed immediately.
Otherwise, they will not be resolved. Defaults to true.

properties.name: isUserVerified
properties.description:
Sets whether User Verification succeeds or fails for an authenticator.
Defaults to false.

properties.name: defaultBackupEligibility
properties.description:
Credentials created by this authenticator will have the backup
eligibility (BE) flag set to this value. Defaults to false.
https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#sctn-credential-backup

properties.name: defaultBackupState
properties.description:
Credentials created by this authenticator will have the backup state
(BS) flag set to this value. Defaults to false.
https://w3c.github.io/webauthn/#sctn-credential-backup

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables the WebMCP domain.

commands.name: enable
commands.description:
Enables the WebMCP domain, allowing events to be sent. Enabling the domain will trigger a toolsAdded event for
all currently registered tools.

events.name: toolInvoked
events.description: Event fired when a tool invocation starts.

parameters.name: toolName
parameters.description: Name of the tool to invoke.

parameters.name: frameId
parameters.description: Frame id

parameters.name: invocationId
parameters.description: Invocation identifier.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The input parameters used for the invocation.

events.name: toolResponded
events.description: Event fired when a tool invocation completes or fails.

parameters.name: invocationId
parameters.description: Invocation identifier.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Status of the invocation.

parameters.name: output
parameters.description: Output or error delivered as delivered to the agent. Missing if `status` is anything other than Success.

parameters.name: errorText
parameters.description: Error text for protocol users.

parameters.name: exception
parameters.description: The exception object, if the javascript tool threw an error>

events.name: toolsAdded
events.description: Event fired when new tools are added.

parameters.name: tools
parameters.description: Array of tools that were added.

events.name: toolsRemoved
events.description: Event fired when tools are removed.

parameters.name: tools
parameters.description: Array of tools that were removed.

types.name: WebMCP.Annotation
types.description: Tool annotations

properties.name: readOnly
properties.description: A hint indicating that the tool does not modify any state.

properties.name: autosubmit
properties.description: If the declarative tool was declared with the autosubmit attribute.

types.name: WebMCP.InvocationStatus
types.description: Represents the status of a tool invocation.

types.name: WebMCP.Tool
types.description: Definition of a tool that can be invoked.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Tool name.

properties.name: description
properties.description: Tool description.

properties.name: inputSchema
properties.description: Schema for the tool's input parameters.

properties.name: annotations
properties.description: Optional annotations for the tool.

properties.name: frameId
properties.description: Frame identifier associated with the tool registration.

properties.name: backendNodeId
properties.description: Optional node ID for declarative tools.

properties.name: stackTrace
properties.description: The stack trace at the time of the registration.
